274	
275	@dataclass
276	class SlateMeta:
277	    slate: dict
278	    force_on: frozenset
279	    force_exact: frozenset
280	    required: tuple
281	    required_off: tuple
282	
283	
284	def load_slate_meta() -> SlateMeta:
285	    """Import the Gate-B run-config constants (the SAME objects the run uses). Lightweight import —
286	    measured ~0.5s, loads ZERO heavy/model modules (verified) — and makes NO network call."""
287	    import scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b as rg  # noqa: PLC0415
288	
289	    return SlateMeta(
290	        slate=dict(rg._FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE),
291	        force_on=frozenset(rg._BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS),
292	        force_exact=frozenset(rg._BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS),
293	        required=tuple(rg._BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS),
294	        required_off=tuple(rg._BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_OFF_FLAGS),
295	    )
296	
297	
298	def resolve_effective_config(env: Mapping[str, str], meta: SlateMeta) -> dict[str, str]:
299	    """READ-ONLY replica of ``apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate``'s env mutation (run_gate_b.py
300	    :2333-2341). For each slate key: force-on / force-exact -> the exact slate value; otherwise a
301	    numeric FLOOR -> ``max(env-or-slate, slate)``. Non-slate keys carry through from ``env`` unchanged.
302	    This is the EFFECTIVE config the --pathB-gate run will have AFTER the slate runs. Pure function —
303	    never writes ``os.environ`` (the source apply() does; this one must not, it is a preflight)."""
304	    eff: dict[str, str] = dict(env)
305	    for name, value in meta.slate.items():
306	        if name in meta.force_on or name in meta.force_exact:
307	            eff[name] = value                              # force exact value (run_gate_b.py:2335)
308	            continue
309	        try:
310	            current = float(env.get(name, value))
311	            eff[name] = str(int(max(current, float(value))))   # FLOOR: raise-to-slate, keep-if-higher
312	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
313	            # P1 resolver-fidelity: mirror apply()'s except branch (run_gate_b.py:2337-2341). On a
314	            # MALFORMED numeric env, production sets ``current = float(value)`` then writes
315	            # ``str(int(max(current, float(value))))`` == the FLOORED SLATE value — NOT the raw env
316	            # string. Replicate that so the preflight cannot false-RED a run that production would
317	            # floor-correct. A genuinely non-numeric SLATE value (which production's float(value)
318	            # would itself crash on, but a preflight must never crash) carries through unchanged.
319	            try:
320	                eff[name] = str(int(float(value)))
321	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
322	                eff[name] = env.get(name, value)
323	    return eff
324	
325	
326	def resolve_generator_slug(env: Mapping[str, str], lock: dict) -> str:
327	    """The generator runs upstream on OpenRouter via openrouter_client (PG_GENERATOR_MODEL >
328	    OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL > lock default) — mirrors pathB_runner + the family-guard precedence."""
329	    return (
330	        env.get("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL")
331	        or env.get("OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL")
332	        or str(lock["required_roles"]["generator"]["model_slug"])
333	    )
334	
335	
336	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
337	# D-1 — FLAGS ON / WIRED
338	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
339	
340	def check_d1_flags(
341	    cfg: Mapping[str, str],
342	    raw_env: Mapping[str, str],
343	    meta: SlateMeta,
344	    four_role_mode_wired: bool,
345	) -> list[CheckResult]:
346	    out: list[CheckResult] = []
347	    # The breadth/quality flags the slate force-pins ON. GREEN requires BOTH effective-truthy AND a
348	    # force-pin (force-on / force-exact / required / numeric-floor) so a stray operator =0 cannot win.
349	    pinned = {
350	        "PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED": "breadth weighted-enrichment surface (the 485->~13 funnel fix)",
351	        "PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_RENDER_VERIFIED_SPANS": "FIX-K: render the surfaced basket's VERIFIED spans verbatim",
352	        "PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE": "B4 relevance gate (weight-not-filter demote-not-drop)",
353	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) WS-2: the two remaining winner flags the paid run must NOT launch with OFF.
354	        # This turns the D-1 launch-path INFO disclosure (a paid run that skips the slate leaves these
355	        # default-OFF -> zero consolidation / zero analysis) into a HARD RED assert on the paid path. The
356	        # real Gate-B slate force-pins + preflight-requires both, so a slate-applied run is GREEN; a
357	        # slate-OFF (or --pathB-gate) launch RED-gates -> NO-GO.
358	        "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI": "W10 consolidate=NLI (same-claim paraphrase baskets -> multi-source corroboration)",
359	        "PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS": "M6 cross-source analytical layer (the Comparative-Assessment analysis yield)",
360	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) DRB-II COVERAGE LEVERS: the 8 weight-and-consolidate breadth levers the Gate-B
361	        # slate now force-ON-pins + preflight-requires. RED-gate a launch that leaves any one OFF (a lever
362	        # running DARK ships a NARROWER report). The real slate governs all 8 (force_on + required + in_slate),
363	        # so a slate-applied run is GREEN; a slate-OFF launch RED-gates -> NO-GO. §-1.3 DNA-ALIGNED (arms the
364	        # existing default; NO forced number); FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL (surfaced/routed sources re-pass strict_verify).
365	        "PG_FACET_OUTLINE": "O1 facet outline (section count emerges from evidence clusters)",
366	        "PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS": "F1 route-every-verified-basket (consolidate-don't-drop the stranded baskets)",
367	        "PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD": "F2 per-section evidence budget tracks full matched payload (ceiling removed)",
368	        "PG_WORD_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD": "F5 per-section word budget tracks full routed payload (clamp removed)",
369	        "PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER": "R1 expert facet planner (widen retrieval breadth)",
370	        "PG_FACET_COMPLETENESS": "R2 facet completeness (retrieval-breadth completeness pass)",
371	        "PG_QUALIFIER_ELABORATION": "D1 within-basket verbatim qualifier elaboration (keep-all; was Gate-B-dark)",
372	        "PG_ENRICHMENT_FACET_ROUTE": "D4 facet-routed enrichment placement (keep-all; was Gate-B-dark)",
373	        "PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE": "the native 4-role D8 verification seam",
374	    }
375	    for flag, why in pinned.items():
376	        if flag == "PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE":
377	            # NOT a slate key — wired by enable_four_role_mode() on the --pathB-gate entry (it force-
378	            # sets os.environ['PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE']='1' unconditionally, run_gate_b.py:241-247).
379	            ok = four_role_mode_wired
380	            detail = (
381	                f"set by enable_four_role_mode() on the --pathB-gate entry (code-wired, force-set '1', "
382	                f"NOT a slate key) -> {'WIRED' if ok else 'NOT wired on the run path'}; "
383	                f"a direct run_honest_sweep_r3.py without --pathB-gate would NOT enable it"
384	            )
385	            out.append(CheckResult("D-1." + flag, why, GREEN if ok else RED, STATIC, detail))
386	            continue
387	        eff = cfg.get(flag)
388	        force_on = flag in meta.force_on
389	        force_exact = flag in meta.force_exact
390	        required = flag in meta.required
391	        in_slate = flag in meta.slate
392	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) WS-2: only RED-gate a pinned winner flag that THIS slate-meta actually
393	        # governs (in the slate or a force/required set). A meta with no knowledge of the flag (a synthetic

505	def _paid_path_winner_slate_on(env: Mapping[str, str]) -> bool:
506	    """Default-ON kill-switch parse, byte-identical to run_honest_sweep_r3.winner_slate_on_paid_path_enabled."""
507	    return env.get(_PAID_PATH_WINNER_SLATE_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "off", "no")

510	def resolve_paid_path_winner_flags(env: Mapping[str, str]) -> dict[str, str]:
511	    """READ-ONLY replica of ``run_honest_sweep_r3.apply_winner_slate_on_paid_path``'s env mutation:
512	    when the kill-switch is ON the launcher force-sets each winner flag ``"1"``; when OFF the flag
513	    keeps its raw env value. Pure — never writes ``os.environ``."""
514	    killswitch_on = _paid_path_winner_slate_on(env)
515	    return {
516	        flag: ("1" if killswitch_on else env.get(flag, ""))
517	        for flag, _why in _PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS
518	    }

633	def ping_openrouter_catalog(slugs: Mapping[str, str], judge_slug: str, th: Thresholds) -> dict:
634	    """FREE unauthenticated GET against the OpenRouter catalog — NO spend, NO completion. Returns a
635	    structured result; ANY network/parse failure -> {'reachable': False, ...} (degrades, never raises)."""
636	    result: dict = {"reachable": False, "served": {}, "judge_providers": None, "error": None}
637	    try:
638	        import httpx  # noqa: PLC0415
639	
640	        base = th.ping_base_url.rstrip("/")
641	        with httpx.Client(timeout=th.ping_timeout_seconds) as client:
642	            resp = client.get(f"{base}/models")
643	            resp.raise_for_status()
644	            data = resp.json().get("data")
645	            if not isinstance(data, list):
646	                raise ValueError("/models body has no `data` list")
647	            catalog: set[str] = set()
648	            for entry in data:
649	                if not isinstance(entry, dict):
650	                    continue
651	                for key in ("id", "canonical_slug"):
652	                    val = entry.get(key)
653	                    if val:
654	                        catalog.add(val)
655	            result["served"] = {role: (slug in catalog) for role, slug in slugs.items()}
656	            # provider count for the Judge (anti-429) = len of /models/{slug}/endpoints.
657	            ep = client.get(f"{base}/models/{judge_slug}/endpoints")
658	            ep.raise_for_status()
659	            ep_data = ep.json().get("data")
660	            endpoints = ep_data.get("endpoints") if isinstance(ep_data, dict) else None
661	            if isinstance(endpoints, list):
662	                result["judge_providers"] = len(endpoints)
663	            result["reachable"] = True
664	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — any failure degrades to LIVE-PENDING, never RED/harness-error
665	        result["error"] = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
666	    return result

711	def _effective_token(cfg: Mapping[str, str], knob: str) -> Optional[int]:
712	    """The effective token budget the run reads: the resolved cfg value if present (env override or
713	    slate floor), else the documented code default."""
714	    if knob in cfg:
715	        return _to_int(cfg.get(knob))
716	    return _CODE_DEFAULT_TOKENS.get(knob)

719	def check_d3_tokens(cfg: Mapping[str, str], th: Thresholds) -> list[CheckResult]:
720	    specs = [
721	        ("generator", "PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS", th.generator_token_floor),
722	        ("judge (D8 verdict)", "PG_D8_VERDICT_MAX_TOKENS", th.judge_token_floor),
723	        ("mirror", "PG_MIRROR_MAX_TOKENS", th.mirror_token_floor),
724	        ("sentinel (decomposition)", "PG_SENTINEL_DECOMPOSITION_MAX_TOKENS", th.sentinel_token_floor),
725	        ("side-judge (NLI entailment)", "PG_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS", th.sidejudge_token_floor),
726	    ]
727	    out: list[CheckResult] = []
728	    for role, knob, floor in specs:
729	        eff = _effective_token(cfg, knob)
730	        if eff is None:
731	            out.append(CheckResult(
732	                f"D-3.{knob}", f"{role} max_tokens >= {floor}", RED, STATIC,
733	                f"{knob}={cfg.get(knob)!r} is not a parseable int (un-resolvable token budget)",
734	            ))
735	            continue
736	        ok = eff >= floor
737	        out.append(_ok(
738	            f"D-3.{knob}", f"{role} max_tokens >= {floor}", ok, STATIC,
739	            f"{knob} effective={eff} (floor {floor}) -> "
740	            f"{'generous (never starves reasoning->verdict)' if ok else 'STARVED: truncates mid-write -> empty/collapse'}",
741	        ))
742	    return out

749	def check_d4_timeouts(cfg: Mapping[str, str], th: Thresholds) -> list[CheckResult]:
750	    gen = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_GENERATOR_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"))
751	    section = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS"))
752	    run_wall = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_SEC"))
753	    seam = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"))
754	    out: list[CheckResult] = []
755	
756	    if None in (gen, section, run_wall):
757	        out.append(CheckResult(
758	            "D-4.ordering", "gen-call-wall < per-section budget < run wall", RED, STATIC,
759	            f"un-resolvable timeout(s): gen={gen} section={section} run_wall={run_wall}",
760	        ))
761	    else:
762	        ordering_ok = gen < section < run_wall
763	        out.append(_ok(
764	            "D-4.ordering", "gen-call-wall < per-section budget < run wall", ordering_ok, STATIC,
765	            f"gen={gen} < section={section} < run_wall={run_wall} -> "
766	            f"{'ordered (no healthy section guillotined mid-write)' if ordering_ok else 'INVERTED -> mid-write truncation'}",
767	        ))
768	
769	    if seam is None or run_wall is None:
770	        out.append(CheckResult(
771	            "D-4.seam", "4-role seam wall raised for the big/slow kimi judge", RED, STATIC,
772	            f"un-resolvable: seam={seam} run_wall={run_wall}",
773	        ))
774	    else:
775	        # Binding lower bound = max(the §9.1.8 sane floor for the slow kimi judge, a single gen call) so
776	        # the seam is never tinier than a generation call AND is generous for the per-claim 4-role burst;
777	        # binding upper bound = the run wall (the seam must terminate inside it). seam-vs-section is
778	        # advisory: the locked full slate legitimately runs seam(7200) < section(9000) because the
779	        # section wall sizes ONE 64000-token section's generation while the seam covers whole-report D8.
780	        lower = max(th.seam_floor_seconds, gen or 0)
781	        seam_ok = (seam >= lower) and (seam <= run_wall)
782	        out.append(_ok(
783	            "D-4.seam", "4-role seam wall sane (>= floor & gen call, <= run wall)", seam_ok, STATIC,
784	            f"seam={seam} (floor {th.seam_floor_seconds}, gen-call {gen}, lower-bound {lower}) "
785	            f"vs run_wall={run_wall}; section={section} (advisory) -> "
786	            f"{'sane: generous for kimi, terminates inside the wall' if seam_ok else 'BAD: too short -> seam tears, or exceeds the run wall'}",
787	        ))
788	    return out

797	
798	    fetch = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP"))
799	    fetch_ok = fetch is not None and fetch >= th.fetch_cap_floor

800	    out.append(_ok(
801	        "D-5.fetch_cap", f"PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP >= {th.fetch_cap_floor} (breadth floor)", fetch_ok, STATIC,
802	        f"PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP effective={fetch} (floor {th.fetch_cap_floor}) -> "
803	        f"{'breadth retained' if fetch_ok else 'STARVED main fetch lane'}",
804	    ))
805	
806	    # The §-1.3-BANNED per-source citation cap was DELETED from fact_dedup.py — it must be ABSENT.
807	    span_cap = raw_env.get("PG_SPAN_PER_SOURCE_CITE_CAP")
808	    span_ok = span_cap is None or span_cap.strip() == ""
809	    out.append(CheckResult(
810	        "D-5.span_cite_cap", "§-1.3-banned PG_SPAN_PER_SOURCE_CITE_CAP is ABSENT",
811	        GREEN if span_ok else RED, STATIC,
812	        f"PG_SPAN_PER_SOURCE_CITE_CAP={span_cap!r} -> "
813	        f"{'absent (the deleted bolt-on stays gone)' if span_ok else 'PRESENT: a banned per-source cite cap is pinned'}",
814	    ))
815	
816	    # PG_CAPPED_FINDING_DEDUP must be 0 on the production path (force-exact '0' in the slate).
817	    capped = cfg.get("PG_CAPPED_FINDING_DEDUP")
818	    capped_ok = not _truthy(capped)
819	    out.append(CheckResult(
820	        "D-5.capped_finding_dedup", "PG_CAPPED_FINDING_DEDUP == 0 (no re-cap-to-max_ev)",
821	        GREEN if capped_ok else RED, STATIC,
822	        f"PG_CAPPED_FINDING_DEDUP effective={capped!r} -> "
823	        f"{'0 (consolidate keep-all; no funnel re-cap)' if capped_ok else 'TRUTHY: the §-1.3-banned re-cap is re-armed'}",
824	    ))
825	
826	    # The historical hidden [:N] choke = the generator-facing pool cap PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN
827	    # (was 20 -> 98% of the corpus silently dropped before per-section selection).
828	    pool = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN"))
829	    pool_ok = pool is not None and pool >= th.gen_pool_floor
830	    out.append(_ok(
831	        "D-5.gen_pool", f"PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN >= {th.gen_pool_floor} (no hidden pool [:N] choke)",
832	        pool_ok, STATIC,
833	        f"PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN effective={pool} (floor {th.gen_pool_floor}) -> "
834	        f"{'full pool reaches per-section selection' if pool_ok else 'CHOKED: rows silently dropped pre-selection'}",
835	    ))
836	    return out
837	
838	
839	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
840	# D-6 — FAIL-LOUD / NO SILENT DEGRADE
841	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
842	
843	def check_d6_fail_loud(cfg: Mapping[str, str], th: Thresholds) -> list[CheckResult]:
844	    out: list[CheckResult] = []
845	    # The disclosure-PRODUCING flags must be effectively ON so a degrade is written to the manifest
846	    # (disclosed) rather than silently swallowed.
847	    disclosures = {
848	        "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN": "credibility tiering_status / corpus_credibility_disclosure to the manifest",
849	        "PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING": "the LLM tiering winner that stamps tiering_status",
850	        "PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE": "relevance_gate demote-not-drop disclosure (demoted_fetched_to_fill)",
851	        "PG_REDACT_HELD_UNSUPPORTED": "held-unsupported quarantine disclosure (not a silent drop)",
852	        "PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE": "always-release + disclosed_gaps (a gap is disclosed, never silently withheld)",
853	    }
854	    for flag, why in disclosures.items():
855	        eff = cfg.get(flag)
856	        ok = _truthy(eff)
857	        out.append(_ok(
858	            f"D-6.{flag}", f"{flag} ON (degrade disclosed, not silent)", ok, STATIC,
859	            f"effective={eff!r} -> {'wired (disclosure reaches the manifest)' if ok else 'OFF: a degrade would be SILENT'}; {why}",
860	        ))
861	    return out
862	
863	
864	def shell_diced_preflight(th: Thresholds) -> CheckResult:
865	    """Shell ``pipeline_diced_preflight.py --json`` READ-ONLY and report its GO/NO-GO as an INPUT.
866	
867	    INFORMATIONAL ONLY — never gates THIS config preflight's verdict. The diced harness gates a
868	    DIFFERENT concern (pipeline-STAGE §-1.3 invariants) and is deliberately CALIBRATED to go RED on the
869	    pre-fix banked drb_72 fixture (its own docstring) plus carries a KNOWN-RED-by-design dice, so its
870	    NO-GO is expected and orthogonal to run-config readiness. Surfaced so the operator sees both lanes;
871	    a missing fixture (exit 2) is reported, not failed. NEVER RED (status INFO)."""
872	    label = "diced pipeline-stage preflight (INFORMATIONAL input — never gates this verdict)"
873	    if not _DICED_PREFLIGHT.is_file():
874	        return CheckResult("D-6.diced", label, INFO, LIVE, f"{_DICED_PREFLIGHT} not found — cannot shell it")
875	    tmp = None
876	    try:
877	        import tempfile  # noqa: PLC0415
878	
879	        fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix="opready_diced_", suffix=".json")
880	        os.close(fd)
881	        proc = subprocess.run(
882	            [sys.executable, str(_DICED_PREFLIGHT), "--json", tmp],
883	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=th.diced_timeout_seconds,
884	            cwd=str(_REPO_ROOT),
885	        )
886	        rc = proc.returncode
887	        verdict = {0: "GO", 1: "NO-GO", 2: "HARNESS-ERROR(no fixture?)"}.get(rc, f"rc={rc}")
888	        red_stages: list[str] = []
889	        by_design: list[str] = []
890	        if tmp and Path(tmp).is_file():
891	            try:
892	                payload = json.loads(Path(tmp).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
893	                red_stages = payload.get("red_eruption_stages") or []
894	                by_design = payload.get("red_by_design_stages") or []
895	            except (OSError, ValueError):
896	                pass
897	        if rc == 2:
898	            return CheckResult(
899	                "D-6.diced", label, INFO, LIVE,
900	                "diced exit=2 (harness error — likely the banked drb_72 fixture is absent on this host); "
901	                "run it where the fixture exists. Informational only.",
902	            )
903	        return CheckResult(
904	            "D-6.diced", label, INFO, LIVE,
905	            f"diced verdict={verdict}; eruption-RED={red_stages or 'none'}; "
906	            f"known-RED-by-design={by_design or 'none'} "
907	            f"(NO-GO here is expected on the pre-fix banked fixture; see the diced table — does NOT gate config readiness)",
908	        )
909	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
910	        return CheckResult("D-6.diced", label, INFO, LIVE,
911	                           f"diced exceeded {th.diced_timeout_seconds}s — skipped (informational)")
912	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
913	        return CheckResult("D-6.diced", label, INFO, LIVE,
914	                           f"could not shell the diced preflight: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
915	    finally:
916	        if tmp and Path(tmp).is_file():
917	            try:
918	                Path(tmp).unlink()
919	            except OSError:
920	                pass
921	
922	
923	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
924	# Orchestration
925	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
926	
927	def run_static_checks(env: Mapping[str, str], th: Thresholds) -> tuple[list[CheckResult], dict]:
928	    """Run every STATIC check. Returns (results, context) where context carries the resolved lineup /
929	    slugs the LIVE ping needs.
930	
931	    The ``run_gate_b`` import + every downstream call run INSIDE ``_process_env_byte_identical`` so the
932	    process env is left byte-identical (P0 fix). When ``env`` is the live ``os.environ`` (the real-run
933	    path), the resolver still sees the .env-merged env WHILE inside the guard (fidelity preserved);
934	    when ``env`` is an injected dict (tests), resolution is unaffected and the guard merely undoes the
935	    import's mutation. Imports run_gate_b (lightweight, no model, no network)."""
936	    with _process_env_byte_identical():
937	        import scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b as rg  # noqa: PLC0415
938	        from src.polaris_graph.roles.openrouter_role_transport import benchmark_verifier_lineup  # noqa: PLC0415
939	
940	        meta = load_slate_meta()
941	        cfg = resolve_effective_config(env, meta)
942	        lock = rg.load_lock()
943	        generator_slug = resolve_generator_slug(env, lock)
944	        lineup = benchmark_verifier_lineup()
945	
946	        # four-role-mode is wired by enable_four_role_mode() on the Gate-B entry — verify the entry
947	        # CALLS it (P2: AST call-node check, not a substring that false-passes on a comment/docstring).
948	        four_role_mode_wired = _source_calls(
949	            inspect.getsource(rg.run_gate_b_query), None, "enable_four_role_mode"
950	        )
951	
952	        families: Optional[dict] = None
953	        families_error: Optional[str] = None
954	        try:
955	            families = rg.assert_four_role_families_distinct()
956	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a config-only collision check; capture, never crash
957	            families_error = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
958	
959	        results: list[CheckResult] = []
960	        results += check_d1_flags(cfg, env, meta, four_role_mode_wired)
961	        results += check_d1_launch_path(rg)
962	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) WS-2: RED-gate a PAID run_honest_sweep_r3.py launch whose winner slate
963	        # is not fully ON (the paid launcher now applies it itself via the default-ON kill-switch).
964	        results += check_d1_paid_path_winner_slate(env)
965	        results += check_d2_models_static(lineup, generator_slug, lock, families, families_error, th)
966	        results += check_d3_tokens(cfg, th)
967	        results += check_d4_timeouts(cfg, th)
968	        results += check_d5_caps(cfg, env, th)
969	        results += check_d6_fail_loud(cfg, th)
970	
971	        slugs = {
972	            "generator": generator_slug,
973	            "mirror": lineup.get("mirror"),
974	            "sentinel": lineup.get("sentinel"),
975	            "judge": lineup.get("judge"),
976	        }
977	        return results, {"slugs": slugs, "judge_slug": lineup.get("judge")}
978	
979	
980	def aggregate(results: list[CheckResult]) -> str:
981	    return "NO-GO" if any(r.is_red for r in results) else "GO"
982	
983	
984	def print_table(results: list[CheckResult], verdict: str) -> None:
985	    id_w = max([len(r.check_id) for r in results] + [12])
986	    print("\n" + "=" * (id_w + 70))
987	    print("  OPERATIONAL-READINESS PREFLIGHT — will-it-die-from-a-stupid-mistake check")
988	    print("=" * (id_w + 70))
989	    print(f"  {'STATUS':<12} {'KIND':<10} {'CHECK':<{id_w}}  WHAT / DETAIL")
990	    print("-" * (id_w + 70))
991	    for r in results:
992	        print(f"  {r.status:<12} {r.static_or_live:<10} {r.check_id:<{id_w}}  {r.what}")
993	        print(f"  {'':<12} {'':<10} {'':<{id_w}}    -> {r.detail}")
994	    print("-" * (id_w + 70))
995	    greens = sum(r.status == GREEN for r in results)
996	    reds = sum(r.is_red for r in results)
997	    pendings = sum(r.is_pending for r in results)
998	    infos = sum(r.is_info for r in results)
999	    print(f"  {greens} GREEN, {reds} RED, {pendings} LIVE-PENDING, {infos} INFO  ->  {verdict}")
1000	    if reds:
1001	        print("  RED (these would kill / cripple the run):")
1002	        for r in results:
1003	            if r.is_red:
1004	                print(f"    - {r.check_id}: {r.detail}")
1005	    if pendings:
1006	        print("  LIVE-PENDING (could not confirm without a live probe; does not force NO-GO):")
1007	        for r in results:
1008	            if r.is_pending:
1009	                print(f"    - {r.check_id}: {r.detail}")
1010	    if infos:
1011	        print("  INFO (surfaced input; does NOT gate the verdict):")
1012	        for r in results:
1013	            if r.is_info:
1014	                print(f"    - {r.check_id}: {r.detail}")
1015	    print("=" * (id_w + 70))
1016	
1017	
1018	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
1019	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Operational-readiness preflight for the Gate-B paid run.")
1020	    ap.add_argument("--json", default=None, help="write a machine-readable result sidecar here.")
1021	    ap.add_argument("--no-ping", action="store_true",
1022	                    help="skip the free OpenRouter /models reachability GET (static checks only).")
1023	    ap.add_argument("--no-diced", action="store_true",
1024	                    help="skip shelling pipeline_diced_preflight.py (informational input).")
1025	    ap.add_argument("--require-live-ping", action="store_true",
1026	                    help="treat an unreachable /models ping as NO-GO (default: LIVE-PENDING).")
1027	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
1028	
1029	    # Render the §/non-ASCII detail correctly regardless of the Windows console codepage (the operator
1030	    # reads by ear; mojibake is noise). Best-effort — never fatal.
1031	    try:
1032	        sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1033	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1034	        pass
1035	
1036	    th = Thresholds()
1037	    try:
1038	        results, ctx = run_static_checks(os.environ, th)
1039	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — any setup/import failure is a HARNESS error (exit 2)
1040	        print(f"[opready] HARNESS ERROR: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
1041	        traceback.print_exc()
1042	        return 2
1043	
1044	    if not args.no_ping:
1045	        ping = ping_openrouter_catalog(ctx["slugs"], ctx["judge_slug"], th)
1046	        results += check_d2_models_live(ping, ctx["slugs"], ctx["judge_slug"], th, args.require_live_ping)
1047	    else:
1048	        results.append(CheckResult(
1049	            "D-2.ping", "OpenRouter /models reachability (skipped)", PENDING, LIVE,
1050	            "--no-ping: skipped the free catalog GET; confirm slug presence + judge providers on the run host",
1051	        ))
1052	
1053	    if not args.no_diced:
1054	        results.append(shell_diced_preflight(th))
1055	    else:
1056	        results.append(CheckResult(
1057	            "D-6.diced", "diced pipeline-stage preflight (skipped)", INFO, LIVE,
1058	            "--no-diced: did not shell pipeline_diced_preflight.py",
1059	        ))
1060	
1061	    verdict = aggregate(results)
1062	    print_table(results, verdict)
1063	
1064	    if args.json:
1065	        payload = {
1066	            "verdict": verdict,
1067	            "thresholds": th.__dict__,
1068	            "checks": [r.__dict__ for r in results],
1069	            "red": [r.check_id for r in results if r.is_red],
1070	            "pending": [r.check_id for r in results if r.is_pending],
1071	        }
1072	        Path(args.json).write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
1073	        print(f"[opready] wrote sidecar -> {args.json}")
1074	
1075	    return 1 if verdict == "NO-GO" else 0
1076	
1077	
1078	if __name__ == "__main__":
1079	    sys.exit(main())

content.content:
1	"""Production-faithful cp3 -> S4 outline lab bank adapter (I-arch section-modular).
2	
3	Builds the offline S4 hamster bank (``bank_plan.json``) from REAL cp2 + cp3 checkpoints — NO
4	synthetic data (LAW II). Pool = cp3 basket members enriched with the REAL cp2 corpus row
5	(title / statement / tier). Clusters = cp3 multi-member baskets. ``same_work_groups`` carried
6	straight from cp3.
7	
8	Item 6a (I-arch s4-outline fix wave): bank rows now CARRY the cp2 content-integrity + topic + weight
9	STAMPS. The prior per-run adapter emitted only ``{evidence_id, title, statement, tier}``, DROPPING
10	the stamps, so the fail-open junk / off-topic / chrome predicates
11	(``junk_deletion_gate.is_row_deletable_offtopic`` and the content-integrity chrome predicate) could
12	never FIRE in the lab — a "Just a moment..." Cloudflare row or a confirmed off-topic source read as
13	normal evidence. Carrying the stamps lets the offline lab reproduce the production gate behaviour.
14	§-1.3.1: the gate KEEPS on any uncertainty (fail-open); a missing stamp is simply absent, which the
15	predicate reads as unjudged => KEEP.
16	
17	LAW VI: all paths are CLI args (no hardcoding). There are no fixed corpus paths in this module.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import argparse
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	from pathlib import Path
25	
26	# item 6a: the cp2 stamp fields the fail-open junk / off-topic / chrome predicates + the weight
27	# channel consume (``junk_deletion_gate`` reads these exact names: content_integrity_junk /
28	# content_integrity_class / topic_off_subject / topic_relevance_verdict / topic_offtopic_demoted /
29	# content_relevance_label / deletion_reason). ``line_screen`` / ``provenance_class`` are the S2
30	# line-screen stamps Fable named. ``url`` / ``source_url`` help the chrome predicate. Any field that
31	# is present on the cp2 row is carried verbatim onto the bank row; an absent field is simply not
32	# added (the predicates treat a missing stamp as unjudged => KEEP, §-1.3.1 fail-open).
33	_STAMP_FIELDS = (
34	    "content_integrity_junk",
35	    "content_integrity_class",
36	    "topic_off_subject",
37	    "topic_relevance_verdict",
38	    "topic_offtopic_demoted",
39	    "content_relevance_label",
40	    "deletion_reason",
41	    "line_screen",
42	    "provenance_class",
43	    "url",
44	    "source_url",
45	)
46	
47	
48	def _carry_stamps(dst: dict, src: dict) -> None:
49	    """Copy any present stamp field from the cp2 row ``src`` onto the bank row ``dst`` (item 6a)."""
50	    for field in _STAMP_FIELDS:
51	        if field in src and src.get(field) is not None:
52	            dst[field] = src[field]
53	
54	
55	def build_bank(
56	    cp2_path: Path,
57	    cp3_path: Path,
58	    out_path: Path,
59	    *,
60	    deliverable: dict | None = None,
61	    scope: dict | None = None,
62	) -> dict:
63	    """Assemble the S4 bank from cp2 + cp3 and write ``out_path``. Returns the bank dict."""
64	    cp2 = json.loads(cp2_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
65	    _cp2_rows = cp2.get("evidence_for_gen", []) or []
66	    cp2_rows_total = len(_cp2_rows)  # item 6: the FULL cp2 candidate pool (pre-basket)
67	    by_id = {str(r.get("evidence_id", "")): r for r in _cp2_rows}
68	
69	    d = json.loads(cp3_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
70	    baskets = d["payload"]["baskets"]
71	    same_work_groups = d["payload"].get("same_work_groups", []) or []
72	    question = d.get("question", "")
73	    domain = d.get("domain", "")
74	    cp3_sha = hashlib.sha256(cp3_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
75	
76	    evidence: list[dict] = []
77	    index_of: dict[str, int] = {}
78	    missing: list[str] = []
79	    dedup_skipped: list[str] = []  # item 9: member ev_ids already banked (duplicate basket references)
80	    for b in baskets:
81	        for ev_id in (b.get("member_evidence_ids", []) or []):
82	            ev_id = str(ev_id)
83	            if ev_id in index_of:
84	                # item 9: a member ev_id already banked (the same source cited by >1 basket, or a
85	                # genuinely duplicated cp3 id). Deduped to ONE bank row (so the S4 digest never sees a
86	                # duplicate evidence_id -> spurious self-alias) and the skip is DISCLOSED below.
87	                dedup_skipped.append(ev_id)
88	                continue
89	            row = by_id.get(ev_id)
90	            if row is None:
91	                # cp2 row genuinely missing — carry the cp3 representative text, no synthetic fill.
92	                missing.append(ev_id)
93	                bank_row = {
94	                    "evidence_id": ev_id,
95	                    "title": str(b.get("representative_statement", "") or ""),
96	                    "statement": "",
97	                    "tier": "UNKNOWN",
98	                }
99	            else:
100	                bank_row = {
101	                    "evidence_id": ev_id,
102	                    "title": str(row.get("title", "") or ""),
103	                    "statement": str(row.get("statement", "") or ""),
104	                    "tier": str(row.get("tier", "") or ""),
105	                }
106	                _carry_stamps(bank_row, row)  # item 6a: real cp2 stamps flow into the lab
107	            index_of[ev_id] = len(evidence)
108	            evidence.append(bank_row)
109	
110	    clusters: list[dict] = []
111	    for b in baskets:
112	        ids = [str(x) for x in (b.get("member_evidence_ids", []) or [])]
113	        if len(ids) < 2:
114	            continue
115	        member_indices = [index_of[i] for i in ids if i in index_of]
116	        rep_id = str(b.get("representative_evidence_id", "") or "")
117	        rep_idx = index_of.get(rep_id, member_indices[0])
118	        clusters.append({
119	            "representative_index": rep_idx,
120	            "member_indices": member_indices,
121	            "corroboration_count": len(member_indices),
122	            "member_hosts": list(b.get("member_hosts", []) or []),
123	        })
124	
125	    # item 6: pool-delta disclosure. The bank pool = cp3 basket MEMBERS only; any cp2 candidate never
126	    # basketed by S3 silently vanishes from the S4 pool. Record the counts + the delta so the drop is
127	    # VISIBLE (never silent). VERIFY on the S3 wheel: prove cp3 basket membership covers 100% of its
128	    # cp2 input, OR that this delta equals the disclosed S2/S3 (off-topic/junk/chrome) deletions.
129	    # item 9: ``cp3_member_dedup_skipped`` = duplicate member references collapsed to one bank row.
130	    bank_rows = len(evidence)
131	    cp2_to_bank_delta = cp2_rows_total - bank_rows
132	    bank = {
133	        "question": question,
134	        "domain": domain,
135	        "cp3_sha": cp3_sha,
136	        "evidence": evidence,
137	        "clusters": clusters,
138	        "same_work_groups": same_work_groups,
139	        "pool_ev_ids": [e["evidence_id"] for e in evidence],
140	        # item 6 (pool-delta disclosure):
141	        "cp2_rows_total": cp2_rows_total,
142	        "bank_rows": bank_rows,
143	        "cp2_to_bank_delta": cp2_to_bank_delta,
144	        # item 9 (duplicate-ev_id disclosure): total dup member refs + distinct ev_ids affected.
145	        "cp3_member_dedup_skipped": len(dedup_skipped),
146	        "cp3_member_dedup_distinct": len(set(dedup_skipped)),
147	    }
148	    if deliverable is not None:
149	        bank["deliverable"] = deliverable
150	    if scope is not None:
151	        bank["scope"] = scope
152	
153	    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
154	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(bank, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
155	    stamped_rows = sum(1 for e in evidence if any(f in e for f in _STAMP_FIELDS))
156	    print(f"wrote {out_path} bytes {out_path.stat().st_size}")
157	    print(f"evidence_rows {len(evidence)} clusters(multi>=2) {len(clusters)} "
158	          f"same_work_groups {len(same_work_groups)} missing_in_cp2 {len(missing)} "
159	          f"stamped_rows {stamped_rows}")
160	    # item 6/9: pool-delta + duplicate-ev_id disclosure (a cp2 candidate never basketed by S3 drops
161	    # out of the S4 pool; the delta must equal disclosed S2/S3 deletions — VERIFY on the S3 wheel).
162	    print(f"cp2_rows_total {cp2_rows_total} bank_rows {bank_rows} cp2_to_bank_delta {cp2_to_bank_delta} "
163	          f"cp3_member_dedup_skipped {len(dedup_skipped)} (distinct {len(set(dedup_skipped))})")
164	    print(f"cp3_sha {cp3_sha[:16]}")
165	    return bank
166	
167	
168	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
169	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
170	        description="cp3 -> S4 outline lab bank adapter (carries cp2 content-integrity/topic stamps)"
171	    )
172	    p.add_argument("--cp2", required=True, type=Path, help="cp2 corpus snapshot JSON")
173	    p.add_argument("--cp3", required=True, type=Path, help="cp3 basket snapshot JSON")
174	    p.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path, help="bank_plan.json output path")
175	    p.add_argument("--deliverable", type=Path, default=None,
176	                   help="optional deliverable spec JSON (required_sections / tone / ...)")
177	    p.add_argument("--scope", type=Path, default=None, help="optional scope spec JSON")
178	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
179	    deliverable = (
180	        json.loads(args.deliverable.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if args.deliverable else None
181	    )
182	    scope = json.loads(args.scope.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if args.scope else None
183	    build_bank(args.cp2, args.cp3, args.out, deliverable=deliverable, scope=scope)
184	    return 0
185	
186	
187	if __name__ == "__main__":
188	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	"""S4 OUTLINE offline hamster harness (Design 5 §8).
2	
3	Runs the S4 orchestrator's DETERMINISTIC segments ALONE on a banked bank-file (LAW II: real
4	fetched rows only; the fixture under tests/fixtures/ is a hand-built branch-coverage miniature,
5	labelled as such — NOT a live corpus). Two OFFLINE modes, both pure (no LLM, no network),
6	seconds per iteration so a defect can be read line-by-line, root-caused, patched, re-run:
7	
8	  digest     — build the basket-digest menu + the ORCH-2 requirements block, print the menu and
9	               a coverage table (every pool row -> basket OR singleton). Proves the 100%-of-pool
10	               honesty invariant on the bank.
11	  apply-dry  — apply a RECORDED reviser op list through parse + apply, print the op verdicts,
12	               the recompose (RE-OPEN) set, the kept-byte-identical set, and deferred/rejected
13	               ops. This is where apply-logic bugs are hunted (Design 5 §8 mode `apply-dry`).
14	
15	The LIVE ``plan`` mode makes ONE real GLM outline call and belongs to the VM hamster (the running
16	generator, box2) — it is NOT runnable offline (no model creds) and refuses loudly rather than
17	faking a call (LAW II). It drives the full S4 outline path on a banked cp3 bank: basket-digest +
18	ORCH-2 requirements block -> live outline -> required-title conform/reorder -> deterministic
19	basket_ids backfill -> orphan check -> cp4 write+load (verdict-leak guarded). ``revise`` (the live
20	reviser leg) still belongs to the compose stage and this offline-first harness refuses it.
21	
22	Bank file shape (JSON): {evidence:[{evidence_id,title,statement,tier},...],
23	clusters:[{representative_index,member_indices,corroboration_count,member_hosts},...],
24	same_work_groups:[{member_evidence_ids,canonical_index,same_work_id},...] (PUSH A, OPTIONAL — the
25	exact cp3 payload shape; absent => the digest is byte-identical to the pre-PUSH-A menu),
26	plans:[{title,focus,ev_ids,basket_ids}], section_results:{title:{...}},
27	reviser_output:{ops:[...],gap_queries:[...],revision_needed:bool}, deliverable:{...}, scope:{...}}.
28	"""
29

67	def _effective_s4_flag_slate(model: str) -> dict[str, str]:
68	    """Item 6: capture the FULL effective S4 knob set for the cp4 checkpoint. Until RunConfig (WP-0b)
69	    lands, these env reads ARE the run config — the checkpoint MUST record what produced it or it
70	    cannot prove its own provenance (the whole point of the checkpoint pivot). Values are EFFECTIVE
71	    (env override OR the same default the reader uses; the two token knobs mirror
72	    ``multi_section_generator``'s ``os.getenv(name, "16384"/"6144")`` readers).
73	
74	    Item 7 (honest reporting): ``PG_OUTLINE_REVISE`` and ``PG_EXTRACT_DELIVERABLE_SPEC`` are read
75	    NOWHERE in the worktree (grep-verified) — they are NO-OPS, so they are deliberately NOT recorded
76	    here; listing them would falsely imply they were validated/active this run."""
77	    return {
78	        "PG_OUTLINE_BASKET_DIGEST": os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_BASKET_DIGEST", "0"),
79	        # Item 3a: coverage-routing arm-state — the cp4 checkpoint MUST record whether the compose
80	        # wheel routes orphan baskets + unassigned high-tier singletons to sections
81	        # (PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS, default-OFF; the S5-compose run slate arms it). Operator law:
82	        # "a winner built but left default-OFF is the still-broken loop root" — so the checkpoint
83	        # proves whether it was armed rather than leaving it invisible.
84	        "PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS": os.getenv("PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS", "0"),
85	        "PG_OUTLINE_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS": os.getenv(
86	            "PG_OUTLINE_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS", str(PG_OUTLINE_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS_DEFAULT)),
87	        "PG_OUTLINE_MIN_MAX_TOKENS": os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_MIN_MAX_TOKENS", "16384"),
88	        "PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS": os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", "6144"),
89	        "PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_ROUNDS": os.getenv(
90	            "PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_ROUNDS", str(PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_ROUNDS_DEFAULT)),
91	        "PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_MAX_RECOMPOSE": os.getenv(
92	            "PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_MAX_RECOMPOSE", str(PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_MAX_RECOMPOSE_DEFAULT)),
93	        "model": str(model),
94	    }

97	def _row_topic_verdict(row: dict) -> str:
98	    """Item 3 (THREE-VALUED): the cp4 per-candidate semantic topic verdict, read from the row's
99	    topic-judge stamps. THREE values so the disclosure never overstates ignorance (395/686 rows this
100	    run carry ``topic_offtopic_demoted``, 445 carry ``content_relevance_label=demoted`` — yet every
101	    candidate read as ``unjudged`` before, starving the S5 router):
102	
103	      "off_subject"   — an AFFIRMATIVE deletable off-topic stamp (the SAME fail-open predicate the
104	                        compose router + run-level junk gate consume; a positive-relevance verdict
105	                        vetoes it). This is the ONLY value the orphan-basket all-members rule treats
106	                        as deletable — deletion semantics UNCHANGED (§-1.3.1 judge-only, fail-open).
107	      "demoted_weight" — a WEIGHT-demote stamp (``topic_offtopic_demoted`` truthy, OR
108	                        ``content_relevance_label`` in {demoted, escalated_demoted}). DISCLOSURE only:
109	                        the row is KEPT and routed; the S5 router now sees it is relevance-demoted.
110	      "unjudged"      — no stamp / any uncertainty / import-or-predicate error (FAIL-OPEN => KEEP).
111	
112	    Zero routing/deletion change — only the disclosure is richer (a demoted row no longer hides as
113	    ``unjudged``)."""
114	    r = row or {}
115	    try:
116	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.junk_deletion_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
117	            is_row_deletable_offtopic,
118	        )
119	        if is_row_deletable_offtopic(r):
120	            return "off_subject"
121	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: a judge/import error never flips a row off-topic
122	        pass
123	    if r.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") or str(
124	        r.get("content_relevance_label", "") or ""
125	    ).strip().lower() in ("demoted", "escalated_demoted"):
126	        return "demoted_weight"
127	    return "unjudged"

139	def _mode_digest(bank: dict) -> int:
140	    evidence = bank.get("evidence", [])
141	    # PUSH A: feed the cp3 same_work_groups so the menu reports WORK-level corroboration and folds
142	    # same-work singleton copies. Absent in the bank => None => byte-identical menu.
143	    menu = build_outline_digest(
144	        evidence, _clusters(bank), same_work_groups=bank.get("same_work_groups")
145	    )
146	    print("=== BASKET-DIGEST MENU ===")
147	    print(menu.render())
148	    print(f"\n[menu] total_chars={menu.total_chars} degraded={menu.degraded} "
149	          f"baskets={len(menu.basket_lines)} singletons={len(menu.singleton_lines)} "
150	          f"singleton_folds={sum(len(v) for v in menu.singleton_alias_ev_ids.values())}")
151	
152	    block = build_requirements_block(bank.get("deliverable"), bank.get("scope"))
153	    print("\n=== ORCH-2 REQUIREMENTS BLOCK ===")
154	    print(block if block else "(empty — no deliverable/scope asks => byte-identical no-append)")
155	
156	    print("\n=== COVERAGE TABLE (100%-of-pool honesty) ===")
157	    covered = menu.covered_ev_ids()
158	    # PUSH A: map each folded alias back to the canonical singleton line it joined, so the table
159	    # still lists 100% of the pool (folded aliases are covered, never dropped).
160	    alias_to_canonical = {
161	        a: canonical
162	        for canonical, aliases in menu.singleton_alias_ev_ids.items()
163	        for a in aliases
164	    }
165	    for row in evidence:
166	        ev_id = str(row.get("evidence_id", ""))
167	        if ev_id in menu.ev_id_to_basket:
168	            where = menu.ev_id_to_basket[ev_id]
169	        elif ev_id in alias_to_canonical:
170	            where = f"singleton(=same-work of {alias_to_canonical[ev_id]})"
171	        else:
172	            where = "singleton"
173	        print(f"  {ev_id:<10} -> {where}")
174	    pool = {str(r.get('evidence_id', '')) for r in evidence if r.get('evidence_id')}
175	    missing = pool - covered
176	    print(f"\n[coverage] pool={len(pool)} covered={len(covered & pool)} missing={sorted(missing)}")
177	    return 0 if not missing else 1
178

180	def _mode_apply_dry(bank: dict) -> int:
181	    plans = bank.get("plans", [])
182	    reviser_output = bank.get("reviser_output")
183	    if reviser_output is None:
184	        print("[apply-dry] bank has no `reviser_output` to replay — nothing to apply.")
185	        return 1
186	    allowed = {str(e) for p in plans for e in (p.get("ev_ids") or [])}
187	    for p in plans:
188	        allowed |= {str(e) for e in (p.get("ev_ids") or [])}
189	    # allow ev_ids referenced by the reviser that live in the pool but not yet on a plan. PUSH A
190	    # part (3): pool_ev_ids carries EVERY member incl. every same-work alias, so a planner/reviser
191	    # reference to a folded alias is never rejected as unknown.
192	    allowed |= {str(e) for e in bank.get("pool_ev_ids", [])}
193	    titles = [str(p.get("title", "")) for p in plans]
194	
195	    outcomes = build_section_outcomes(
196	        plans, bank.get("section_results", {}),
197	        basket_members=bank.get("basket_members", {}),
198	        basket_corroboration=bank.get("basket_corroboration", {}),
199	    )
200	    orphans = find_orphan_baskets(plans, bank.get("basket_corroboration", {}))
201	    print("=== SECTION OUTCOME DIGESTS (the section checklist) ===")
202	    for oc in outcomes:
203	        print(f"  {oc.title!r}: verified={oc.verified_sentence_count} kept={oc.kept_fraction} "
204	              f"dropped={oc.dropped} unused={oc.unused_ev_ids} uncovered={oc.uncovered_baskets} "
205	              f"undersupplied={oc.undersupplied}")
206	    print(f"[orphan_baskets] {orphans}")
207	
208	    sigs_before = {str(p.get('title', '')): plan_signature(p) for p in plans}
209	    parsed = parse_revision_ops(reviser_output, allowed_ev_ids=allowed, plan_titles=titles)
210	    # item 13: when the bank has a required-section structure, thread it so apply restricts ops to
211	    # keep/reassign and cannot break the user's exact-N-in-order contract.
212	    _required = [
213	        str(t).strip()
214	        for t in ((bank.get("deliverable") or {}).get("required_sections", []) or [])
215	        if str(t).strip()
216	    ]
217	    applied = apply_revision_ops(plans, parsed, outcomes=outcomes, required_titles=_required)
218	
219	    print("\n=== APPLY RESULT ===")
220	    print(f"[parse] accepted={len(parsed.ops)} rejected={len(parsed.rejected)} "
221	          f"parse_failed={parsed.parse_failed} revision_needed={parsed.revision_needed}")
222	    for rej in parsed.rejected:
223	        print(f"   REJECT {rej.get('reason_code')}: {rej.get('op')}")
224	    print(f"[apply] changed={applied.changed} recompose={applied.recompose_titles} "
225	          f"kept={applied.kept_titles} deferred={len(applied.deferred_ops)}")
226	    print("\n[keep byte-identity] (kept sections must hash-equal wave-1):")
227	    for title in applied.kept_titles:
228	        after = next((plan_signature(p) for p in applied.new_plans
229	                      if str(p.get('title', '')) == title), None)
230	        same = "OK" if sigs_before.get(title) == after else "MISMATCH"
231	        print(f"   {title!r}: {same}")
232	    print("\n=== FINAL PLANS ===")
233	    for p in applied.new_plans:
234	        print(f"  {p.get('title')!r} ev_ids={p.get('ev_ids')} archetype={p.get('archetype')}")
235	    return 0
