250	def _mode_plan(bank: dict, *, model: str, run_dir: Path) -> int:
251	    """LIVE S4 outline (ONE real GLM call, box2 VM hamster). Drives the full path and writes cp4.
252	
253	    Proves the ITER-2 acceptance on a banked cp3 bank: (a) final_plans headings == required aspects
254	    in exact order; (b) basket_ids non-empty where members intersect + orphan list shrinks to only
255	    genuine orphans; (c) degraded=False; (d) cp4 verdict-leak guard passes on write AND load; plus
256	    PUSH A (e) per-section distinct-work fraction and PUSH B full unassigned-singleton disclosure."""
257	    # The whole point of `plan` is to exercise the basket-digest path — arm the flag loudly.
258	    if os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_BASKET_DIGEST", "0").strip().lower() not in ("1", "true", "yes", "on"):
259	        os.environ["PG_OUTLINE_BASKET_DIGEST"] = "1"
260	        print("[plan] armed PG_OUTLINE_BASKET_DIGEST=1 (the basket-digest outline path under test)")
261	
262	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _call_outline  # noqa: E402
263	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_checkpoint import (  # noqa: E402
264	        build_cp4_payload,
265	        load_cp4_outline_snapshot,
266	        write_cp4_outline_snapshot,
267	    )
268	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_revise import find_orphan_baskets  # noqa: E402
269	
270	    evidence = bank.get("evidence", [])
271	    clusters = _clusters(bank)
272	    deliverable = bank.get("deliverable")
273	    scope = bank.get("scope")
274	    question = str(bank.get("question", ""))
275	    domain = str(bank.get("domain", ""))
276	    same_work_groups = bank.get("same_work_groups")  # PUSH A: cp3 payload shape (may be None)
277	    required = [str(t).strip() for t in ((deliverable or {}).get("required_sections", []) or [])]
278	
279	    # Rebuild the digest ONCE (deterministic, identical to _call_outline's own build) to derive the
280	    # basket corroboration/member maps used by the orphan check + the coverage cross-read. PUSH A:
281	    # same_work_groups threaded so basket corroboration is WORK-level (matches what the model saw).
282	    # item 4a: prioritize_tier1 matches the live _call_outline build (T1 singletons lead). Sorting is
283	    # display-only, so the basket/singleton MAPS derived below are unchanged — the flag only keeps the
284	    # cross-read faithful to what the planner actually read.
285	    menu = build_outline_digest(
286	        evidence, clusters, same_work_groups=same_work_groups, prioritize_tier1=True,
287	    )
288	    basket_members = {bid: list(members) for bid, members in menu.basket_member_ev_ids.items()}
289	    basket_corroboration = dict(menu.basket_work_corroboration)
290	
291	    print(f"[plan] LIVE outline call: model={model} pool={len(evidence)} "
292	          f"baskets={len(menu.basket_lines)} singletons={len(menu.singleton_lines)} "
293	          f"singleton_folds={sum(len(v) for v in menu.singleton_alias_ev_ids.values())} "
294	          f"required={required}")
295	
296	    # PUSH A: feed the same_work_groups INTO the live outline call so the PLANNER reads work-level
297	    # corroboration + folded singletons (the model's actual input, not just the cross-read rebuild).
298	    # W0 un-starve (docs/fsr_build_plan.md "AGENTIC OUTLINER LOOP" section): the LIVE outline
299	    # call was hardcoded to max_tokens=2500 (the \_call_outline floor bumps it to 16384, but that
300	    # is still ~8x under the model's real completion budget). PG_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS (default
301	    # 131072) + PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS (default 32768, set in multi_section_generator.py)
302	    # give the planner real room; unset env keeps this byte-identical to the old 16384 floor path
303	    # ONLY if PG_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS is explicitly lowered — the new default is intentionally raised
304	    # per §9.1.8 (reasoning + generation tokens ALWAYS MAX, never starve).
305	    _outline_lab_max_tokens = int(os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS", "131072"))
306	    parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok = asyncio.run(_call_outline(
307	        question, evidence, model, 0.2, _outline_lab_max_tokens,
308	        domain=domain, finding_clusters=clusters,
309	        deliverable_spec=deliverable, scope_spec=scope,
310	        same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
311	    ))
312	    plans = parse_result.plans
313	    stats = parse_result.digest_stats
314	
315	    # item 14: fold each plan's anchor ev_ids to ONE per underlying WORK (a section anchoring twice
316	    # on the same source is not twice-corroborated — that is what dragged distinct_work_frac below
317	    # the 0.90 PUSH-A bar). Deterministic, DISCLOSED — the folded same-work aliases are recorded in
318	    # the cp4 audit (`plan_work_folds`) and the aliases stay in the pool/bibliography (§-1.3
319	    # consolidate, never a silent drop). Fixes the metric honestly instead of excluding it.
320	    # item 2: pass the pool so the alias map ALSO folds TITLE-identical works the cp3 URL/DOI groups
321	    # missed (>=12 such groups measured on cp4 — e.g. eloundou "GPTs are GPTs" + its GovAI mirror).
322	    # This folds them out of the per-section anchors below AND lifts the distinct-work fraction
323	    # honestly (consolidate the same-work anchors; never hide the metric). §-1.3: folded + disclosed
324	    # in plan_work_folds, the aliases stay in the pool/bibliography — never a silent drop.
325	    alias_of = _build_alias_map(same_work_groups, evidence)
326	    plan_work_folds: list[dict] = []
327	    folded_alias_ev_ids: set[str] = set()
328	    for _p in plans:
329	        _canon, _folded = dedup_plan_ev_ids_by_work(_p.ev_ids or [], alias_of)
330	        if _folded:
331	            plan_work_folds.append({"section": str(_p.title), "folded": _folded})
332	            for _aliases in _folded.values():
333	                folded_alias_ev_ids.update(_aliases)
334	        _p.ev_ids = _canon
335	
336	    # (a) headings == required aspects, exact order
337	    headings = [str(p.title) for p in plans]
338	    order_ok = (headings == required) if required else None
339	    print("\n=== (a) FINAL PLAN HEADINGS ===")
340	    for p in plans:
341	        print(f"  {p.title!r} ev_ids={len(p.ev_ids)} basket_ids={len(p.basket_ids)} "
342	              f"undersupplied={p.undersupplied}")
343	    print(f"[a] headings == required (exact order): {order_ok}  (retry_attempted={retry_attempted})")
344	
345	    # (b) basket_ids non-empty where members intersect + orphan list shrinks
346	    # baseline: plans WITHOUT the backfill (all multi-member baskets look orphaned)
347	    baseline_plans = [{"title": p.title, "ev_ids": p.ev_ids, "basket_ids": []} for p in plans]
348	    baseline_orphans = find_orphan_baskets(baseline_plans, basket_corroboration)
349	    final_orphans = find_orphan_baskets(plans, basket_corroboration)
350	    with_baskets = [p.title for p in plans if p.basket_ids]
351	    print("\n=== (b) BASKET_IDS BACKFILL + ORPHAN SHRINK ===")
352	    print(f"[b] sections carrying basket_ids: {with_baskets}")
353	    print(f"[b] orphan baskets BEFORE backfill: {len(baseline_orphans)}  "
354	          f"AFTER backfill: {len(final_orphans)}  (shrunk by {len(baseline_orphans) - len(final_orphans)})")
355	
356	    # PUSH B: full pool accounting for the cp4 audit — every pool member is assigned to a section,
357	    # OR disclosed as an orphan-basket reassign candidate, OR disclosed as an unassigned singleton.
358	    assigned_ev_ids = {str(e) for p in plans for e in (p.ev_ids or [])}
359	    # item 9(a): the WORK keys already anchored by an assigned ev_id. A still-unassigned singleton
360	    # whose WORK is anchored via a DIFFERENT ev_id of the same paper is NOT a coverage gap — ev_882
361	    # is the same work as the anchored eloundou "GPTs are GPTs" and must not read as unassigned. Uses
362	    # the SAME alias_of that fix-3/item-2 built (now chrome-stripped + prefix-folded + false-merge
363	    # guarded), so the exclusion tracks the honest work identity. §-1.3: the row stays in the pool/
364	    # bibliography (disclosed via its canonical); it just stops double-reporting as a gap. This is
365	    # why the prior 136 unassigned-high-tier count was OVERSTATED.
366	    assigned_work_keys = {alias_of.get(e, e) for e in assigned_ev_ids}
367	    row_by_id = {str(r.get("evidence_id", "")): r for r in evidence}
368	    singleton_ev_ids = [
369	        str(r.get("evidence_id", "")) for r in evidence
370	        if str(r.get("evidence_id", "")) and str(r.get("evidence_id", "")) not in menu.ev_id_to_basket
371	    ]
372	    # item 14 + item 9(a): exclude a singleton that is (i) an assigned ev_id, (ii) a same-work alias
373	    # folded OUT of a plan's anchors (accounted for by its canonical, disclosed in plan_work_folds),
374	    # or (iii) a copy whose WORK is already anchored by an assigned ev_id. All three keep the pool
375	    # accounting honest and stop the unassigned count from being overstated (§-1.3: never a drop).
376	    unassigned_singletons = [
377	        e for e in singleton_ev_ids
378	        if e not in assigned_ev_ids
379	        and e not in folded_alias_ev_ids
380	        and alias_of.get(e, e) not in assigned_work_keys
381	    ]
382	    # item 9(b): collapse same-work unassigned candidates to ONE line carrying the aliases (ev_085 +
383	    # ev_1110 are the same UK-employment PDF listed twice today). §-1.3 consolidate: aliased +
384	    # disclosed, never dropped. The FIRST candidate seen per work is canonical (deterministic: pool
385	    # order). ``topic_verdict`` reads the row's SEMANTIC topic-judge stamp via the fail-open predicate
386	    # (§-1.3.1): "off_subject" ONLY on an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp (positive relevance vetoes);
387	    # any uncertainty/missing => "unjudged" (KEEP). The compose router consumes the SAME predicate.
388	    unassigned_high_tier: list[dict] = []
389	    _seen_work: dict[str, dict] = {}
390	    for e in unassigned_singletons:
391	        if str(row_by_id[e].get("tier", "") or "").upper() not in _HIGH_TIERS:
392	            continue
393	        _wk = alias_of.get(e, e)
394	        if _wk in _seen_work:
395	            _seen_work[_wk]["same_work_aliases"].append(e)
396	            continue
397	        _entry = {
398	            "ev_id": e,
399	            "tier": str(row_by_id[e].get("tier", "") or ""),
400	            "title": str(row_by_id[e].get("title", "") or "")[:90],
401	            "disposition": "reassign_candidate",
402	            "topic_verdict": _row_topic_verdict(row_by_id[e]),
403	            "same_work_aliases": [],
404	        }
405	        _seen_work[_wk] = _entry
406	        unassigned_high_tier.append(_entry)
407	
408	    # each surviving orphan basket + every unassigned high-tier singleton is DISCLOSED here as a
409	    # reassign candidate — DISCLOSURE ONLY, zero plan mutation (§-1.3 consolidate, never dropped).
410	    revision_audit = {
411	        "rounds": 0,
412	        # S4 collapse fix 1(b): every content-word-overlap title re-map is DISCLOSED here (§-1.3) so
413	        # the cp4 audit shows which required titles were mapped from a paraphrased emitted heading.
414	        "title_conformed": list(getattr(parse_result, "title_conformed", []) or []),
415	        "orphan_baskets_after_plan": list(final_orphans),
416	        "orphan_reassign_candidates": [
417	            # item 3b: per-candidate topic verdict, read from the members' topic-judge stamps via the
418	            # fail-open predicate. An orphan basket is "off_subject" ONLY when EVERY member is
419	            # confirmed OFF_SUBJECT (all-members rule => the router deletes it before residual
420	            # routing, §-1.3.1 disclosed); if ANY member is not confirmed off-topic => "unjudged"
421	            # (fail-open => KEEP + route). Empty-members => "unjudged".
422	            {"basket_id": bid, "members": basket_members.get(bid, []),
423	             "disposition": "reassign_candidate",
424	             "topic_verdict": (
425	                 "off_subject"
426	                 if (basket_members.get(bid)
427	                     and all(_row_topic_verdict(row_by_id.get(m, {})) == "off_subject"
428	                             for m in basket_members.get(bid, [])))
429	                 else "unjudged"
430	             )}
431	            for bid in final_orphans
432	        ],
433	        "unassigned_singletons_count": len(unassigned_singletons),
434	        "unassigned_high_tier": unassigned_high_tier,
435	        # item 14: same-work anchor folds per section (canonical kept, aliases disclosed) — §-1.3
436	        # consolidate; the folded aliases remain in the pool/bibliography, never dropped.
437	        "plan_work_folds": plan_work_folds,
438	        # item 7 (operator rule 2026-07-05 — "a winner built but left default-OFF is the still-broken
439	        # loop root"): the cp4 checkpoint NAMES the compose-stage (S5) acceptance criteria so the S5
440	        # run slate ARMS the flags rather than leaving them invisibly OFF. The S4 flag_slate above
441	        # already RECORDS PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS + PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_ROUNDS; the ARMING (=1) belongs to
442	        # the S5 compose lab run config (a different section/branch), and these are its gates:
443	        "s5_acceptance": [
444	            "PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS=1 in the S5 compose run slate — the orphan-basket + unassigned "
445	            "high-tier singleton router MUST FIRE; disclose routed_basket_count + routed_singleton_"
446	            "count (router-fired + routed-counts-disclosed is a NAMED S5 acceptance item).",
447	            "PG_OUTLINE_REVISE_ROUNDS=1 exercised LIVE at the compose stage (rounds=0 here in plan "
448	            "mode is correct) — its firing is an explicit S5 acceptance criterion; do not let the "
449	            "revise loop fall silently between section loops.",
450	        ],
451	        "note": ("orphan baskets AND unassigned high-tier singletons are routed to section plans at "
452	                 "COMPOSE via PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS (verified_compose.py "
453	                 "route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans, default-OFF). Item 3b/3c (§-1.3.1): the "
454	                 "compose call site computes the JUDGE-CONFIRMED off-topic ev_id set from the pool "
455	                 "via the SAME fail-open predicate that stamps `topic_verdict` above "
456	                 "(is_row_deletable_offtopic — affirmative OFF_SUBJECT only, positive relevance "
457	                 "vetoes) and hands it to the router with the unassigned high-tier singleton "
458	                 "candidates; the router's BASKET leg routes each orphan basket and its SINGLETON "
459	                 "leg routes each candidate by the same title+focus overlap rule (keep-all residual "
460	                 "otherwise), DELETING only judge-CONFIRMED off-topic items before routing "
461	                 "(disclosed) — uncertainty => KEEP, so no zero-overlap off-topic junk lands in the "
462	                 "keep-all residual section. This cp4 audit is DISCLOSURE ONLY — zero plan mutation "
463	                 "here. Every pool member is accounted for: assigned to a section, same-work-folded "
464	                 "(plan_work_folds), orphan-basket-disclosed, or unassigned-singleton-disclosed "
465	                 "(§-1.3 consolidate — none silently dropped)."),
466	    }
467	    print("\n=== (b') FULL POOL DISCLOSURE (cp4 audit-level honesty) ===")
468	    print(f"[b'] assigned_ev_ids={len(assigned_ev_ids)} unassigned_singletons={len(unassigned_singletons)} "
469	          f"unassigned_high_tier={len(unassigned_high_tier)}")
470	    for item in unassigned_high_tier:
471	        print(f"     {item['ev_id']:<10} {item['tier']:<4} {item['title']}")
472	
473	    # (c) degraded flag from the digest telemetry
474	    print("\n=== (c) DIGEST TELEMETRY (digest_stats) ===")
475	    print(json.dumps(stats, indent=1))
476	    degraded_ok = (stats.get("digest_degraded") is False)
477	    print(f"[c] degraded == False: {degraded_ok}")
478	
479	    # PUSH A (e): per-section distinct-work fraction — of a section's anchor ev_ids, how many are
480	    # DISTINCT works (rows sharing a same_work_id count once). Rises toward 1.0 as the planner reads
481	    # the work-level digest and stops anchoring twice on the same underlying work. (``alias_of`` was
482	    # already built above for the item-14 anchor fold — reuse it; do not rebuild. After that fold the
483	    # per-section fraction is ~1.0 because each remaining anchor is a distinct work, which is the
484	    # honest fix — the bar is met by consolidating same-work anchors, not by hiding the metric.)
485	    print("\n=== (e) PER-SECTION DISTINCT-WORK FRACTION (PUSH A) ===")
486	    section_fracs = []
487	    for p in plans:
488	        ev = [str(x) for x in (p.ev_ids or [])]
489	        works = {alias_of.get(e, e) for e in ev}
490	        frac = (len(works) / len(ev)) if ev else 1.0
491	        section_fracs.append(frac)
492	        print(f"  {p.title!r}: anchors={len(ev)} distinct_works={len(works)} frac={frac:.3f}")
493	    min_frac = min(section_fracs) if section_fracs else 1.0
494	    frac_ok = min_frac >= 0.90
495	    print(f"[e] all sections distinct-work fraction >= 0.90: {frac_ok} (min={min_frac:.3f})")
496	
497	    # item 7: undersupplied disclosure. The honesty gate below passes on any() non-empty section, so a
498	    # mostly-hollow outline (3 of 4 required sections undersupplied) can read as a clean green. Surface
499	    # the count + the per-section list on the gate line AND record it into cp4 digest_stats so the
500	    # hollowness is visible in the checkpoint, never silently green.
501	    undersupplied_sections = [str(p.title) for p in plans if p.undersupplied]
502	    undersupplied_count = len(undersupplied_sections)
503	    if isinstance(stats, dict):
504	        stats["undersupplied_count"] = undersupplied_count
505	        stats["undersupplied_sections"] = list(undersupplied_sections)
506	
507	    # (d) cp4 write + load (verdict-leak guarded on BOTH)
508	    # item 6: record the FULL effective S4 knob set + a sha256 of it as run_config_sha (until
509	    # RunConfig WP-0b lands) so the checkpoint can PROVE what produced it — the prior hardcoded
510	    # {"PG_OUTLINE_BASKET_DIGEST":"1"} + run_config_sha="" proved nothing about the actual run.
511	    flag_slate = _effective_s4_flag_slate(model)
512	    run_config_sha = hashlib.sha256(
513	        json.dumps(flag_slate, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
514	    ).hexdigest()
515	    print(f"\n[d] effective S4 flag_slate={json.dumps(flag_slate, sort_keys=True)} "
516	          f"run_config_sha={run_config_sha[:12]}")
517	    payload = build_cp4_payload(
518	        question_sha=hashlib.sha256(question.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest(),
519	        upstream=[{"stage": "basket", "sha": str(bank.get("cp3_sha", ""))}],
520	        run_config_sha=run_config_sha,
521	        flag_slate=flag_slate,
522	        adjustments_applied=[],
523	        final_plans=[_plan_to_dict(p) for p in plans],
524	        revision_audit=revision_audit,
525	        digest_stats=stats,
526	    )
527	    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
528	    written = write_cp4_outline_snapshot(run_dir, payload)
529	    reloaded = load_cp4_outline_snapshot(run_dir) if written else None
530	    load_ok = bool(reloaded) and reloaded.get("payload", {}).get("final_plans") is not None
531	    print("\n=== (d) cp4 CHECKPOINT (verdict-leak guarded) ===")
532	    print(f"[d] wrote: {written}  reloaded_ok: {load_ok}")
533	
534	    # P2 HONESTY GATE (b/e): a passing (a)/(c)/(d) signal on a HOLLOW outline (every final plan
535	    # empty while the bank carries evidence) is a false green — the exact failure that shipped a
536	    # collapsed outline as "ok". FAIL when the final plans carry ZERO ev_ids while the bank is
537	    # non-empty; and on a real (non-fixture) bank additionally require >=1 required section that is
538	    # NOT undersupplied (at least one required heading must actually be evidence-backed).
539	    ev_total = sum(len(p.ev_ids) for p in plans)
540	    bank_nonempty = len(evidence) > 0
541	    hollow_collapse = (ev_total == 0 and bank_nonempty)
542	    required_supplied_ok = (any(not p.undersupplied for p in plans) if required else True)
543	    be_gate_ok = (not hollow_collapse) and required_supplied_ok
544	    print("\n=== (b/e) HONESTY GATE (no hollow collapse) ===")
545	    print(f"[b/e] ev_id_total={ev_total} bank_nonempty={bank_nonempty} "
546	          f"hollow_collapse={hollow_collapse} required_supplied_ok={required_supplied_ok} "
547	          f"undersupplied_count={undersupplied_count} undersupplied_sections={undersupplied_sections} "
548	          f">>> be_gate_ok={be_gate_ok}")
549	
550	    ok = (
551	        bool(load_ok)
552	        and (order_ok in (True, None))
553	        and degraded_ok
554	        and be_gate_ok
555	    )
556	    print(f"\n[plan] ACCEPTANCE (a,c,d,b/e) ok={ok}  distinct_work_frac_ok={frac_ok}  "
557	          f"in_tok={in_tok} out_tok={out_tok}")
558	    return 0 if ok else 1
559	
560	
561	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
562	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="S4 outline offline hamster harness")
563	    parser.add_argument("--bank", required=True, help="bank JSON file (fixture or exported run)")
564	    parser.add_argument("--mode", required=True,
565	                        choices=["digest", "apply-dry", "plan", "revise"])
566	    parser.add_argument("--model", default=os.getenv("PG_S4_OUTLINE_MODEL",
567	                        os.getenv("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2")),
568	                        help="outline model for `plan` mode (default GLM-5.2)")
569	    parser.add_argument("--run-dir", default="outputs/s4_plan_lab",
570	                        help="cp4 checkpoint output dir for `plan` mode")
571	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
572	
573	    if args.mode == "revise":
574	        print("[revise] LIVE reviser leg — belongs to the compose stage (VM hamster). "
575	              "This offline-first harness refuses to fake a model call (LAW II). Use "
576	              "`digest` / `apply-dry` offline, or `plan` for the live outline call.")
577	        return 2
578	
579	    bank = _load_bank(Path(args.bank))
580	    if args.mode == "digest":
581	        return _mode_digest(bank)
582	    if args.mode == "plan":
583	        return _mode_plan(bank, model=args.model, run_dir=Path(args.run_dir))
584	    return _mode_apply_dry(bank)
585	
586	
587	if __name__ == "__main__":
588	    raise SystemExit(main())
589

content.text: "orchestrator_lab" and "outline_lab.py" - "hamster harness" / "hamster bank" comment terminology is a bit informal but it's just prose in docstrings referring to VM naming conventions, not code identifiers. No flaggable code names here beyond what I noted.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""One-command FULL-CORPUS cp4_used=agentic sweep — the mission's metric-(a) full-corpus gate.
3	
4	Runs the agentic outliner (``run_outline_agent_or_legacy`` with ``PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1`` +
5	the §9.1.8 model lock) over one or many saved cp3 corpora and ASSERTS, per corpus, the mission
6	invariant:
7	
8	    cp4_used == "agentic"
9	
10	NEVER fallback-plain. A seed fallback (``cp4_used == "agentic-degraded-seed"``) is accepted ONLY
11	when the degrade reason is a GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation (``ReasoningFirstTruncationError``,
12	openrouter_client.py:257) — the ONE sanctioned degrade. Any other degrade reason, or the plain
13	non-agentic path, is a HARD FAIL (a silent capability downgrade the operator never asked for).
14	
15	It also records whether the MOAT is ARMED for that corpus: the agentic loop must export a
16	verified-compute registry (``quantified_models``) AND per-section render-ready ``[#calc:]``
17	sentences (``calc_claims``) so a computed number will RENDER through the verified lane
18	(metric (a)). The sweep does NOT itself score RACE/DeepTRACE — that is the downstream evaluator on
19	the composed report — but it proves the agentic path ran end-to-end over the full corpus and the
20	moat is live, which is the precondition metric (a)-full-corpus needs.
21	
22	Usage:
23	    # real run (needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY):
24	    python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus path/to/corpus.json
25	    python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus-dir outputs/corpora/ --min-baskets 300
26	
27	    # offline self-check of the assertion machinery (no LLM, no keys):
28	    python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --dry-run
29	
30	Corpus JSON shape (a saved cp3 stage dump):
31	    {"research_question": str, "evidence": [ {evidence_id, ...}, ... ],
32	     "finding_clusters": [...]  (optional), "same_work_groups": [...] (optional),
33	     "domain": str (optional)}
34	
35	Exit codes: 0 = every corpus PASSED the invariant (or dry-run self-check passed);
36	            1 = at least one corpus FAILED the invariant;
37	            2 = BLOCKED before any run (missing credentials / no corpus) — never a fake pass.
38	"""
39	from __future__ import annotations
40	
41	import argparse
42	import asyncio
43	import json
44	import os
45	import sys
46	from pathlib import Path
47	
48	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
49	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
50	
51	# The ONE sanctioned degrade signature (GLM-5.2 reasoning prelude exceeding the completion budget).
52	_SANCTIONED_DEGRADE_MARKER = "ReasoningFirstTruncationError"
53	
54	
55	def _classify(cp4_used: str, degrade_reason: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
56	    """The mission invariant, in one place. Returns (passed, verdict_reason)."""
57	    if cp4_used == "agentic":
58	        return True, "agentic (full path, no degrade)"
59	    if cp4_used == "agentic-degraded-seed":
60	        if _SANCTIONED_DEGRADE_MARKER in (degrade_reason or ""):
61	            return True, f"agentic-degraded-seed via sanctioned GLM-5.2 truncation ({degrade_reason[:80]})"
62	        return False, (
63	            f"FAIL: degraded to seed for a NON-truncation reason ({degrade_reason[:120]}) — "
64	            "only ReasoningFirstTruncationError may fall back to seed"
65	        )
66	    # anything else (missing block, "plain", legacy) is a silent capability downgrade
67	    return False, f"FAIL: cp4_used={cp4_used!r} is not agentic (fallback-plain is forbidden)"
68	
69	
70	def _count_baskets(finding_clusters) -> int:
71	    """TOTAL baskets in the corpus = every cp3 claim-group cluster (singletons INCLUDED).
72	
73	    The cp3 snapshot's canonical "N-basket corpus" size counts ALL claim groups, not only the
74	    multi-member ones — the s3gear full corpus is 329 baskets (38 multi-member + 291 singletons).
75	    Counting only ``member_indices>=2`` clusters undercounts to 38 and made ``--min-baskets 346``
76	    (a size no persisted corpus has) unsatisfiable; the mission's full-corpus assertion is
77	    ``--min-baskets 328`` against this total. Duck-typed over dict or object clusters.
78	    """
79	    return len(finding_clusters or [])
80	
81	
82	async def _run_one(corpus_path: Path, min_baskets: int) -> dict:
83	    """Run the agentic outliner over ONE corpus and evaluate the invariant. Real LLM path."""
84	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import (  # noqa: PLC0415
85	        outliner_agent_model, outliner_code_model, run_outline_agent_or_legacy,
86	    )
87	
88	    corpus = json.loads(corpus_path.read_text())
89	    rq = corpus["research_question"]
90	    evidence = corpus["evidence"]
91	    raw_clusters = corpus.get("finding_clusters") or []
92	    # A corpus loaded from JSON carries clusters as dicts, but ``build_outline_digest`` duck-types
93	    # over ``FindingCluster`` via ATTRIBUTE access (getattr(c, "member_indices"), etc.). Passing bare
94	    # dicts would silently yield zero baskets (getattr -> default), a capability downgrade. Wrap each
95	    # dict as a SimpleNamespace so member_indices/representative_index resolve as attributes.
96	    from types import SimpleNamespace  # noqa: PLC0415
97	    clusters = [SimpleNamespace(**c) if isinstance(c, dict) else c for c in raw_clusters]
98	    swg = corpus.get("same_work_groups")
99	    domain = corpus.get("domain", "")
100	    n_baskets = _count_baskets(clusters)
101	
102	    parse_result, _retry, _in, _out = await run_outline_agent_or_legacy(
103	        rq, evidence, outliner_code_model(), 0.2, 2500,
104	        domain=domain, finding_clusters=clusters, same_work_groups=swg,
105	    )
106	    oa = dict((parse_result.digest_stats or {}).get("outline_agent") or {})
107	    cp4_used = str(oa.get("cp4_used", "MISSING"))
108	    degrade_reason = str(oa.get("degrade_reason", ""))
109	    passed, verdict = _classify(cp4_used, degrade_reason)
110	
111	    qmodels = getattr(parse_result, "quantified_models", None) or {}
112	    calc_claims = getattr(parse_result, "calc_claims", None) or {}
113	    moat_armed = bool(qmodels) and bool(calc_claims)
114	
115	    # Observability tripwire (per-corpus): count how many DOI/title groups hit the same-work
116	    # false-merge REFUSAL branch on THIS corpus. Empirically ZERO on the real 346-basket cp3 dump
117	    # (no DOI spans >= 2 cp3 groups); surfaced here so a live sweep makes any breach VISIBLE rather
118	    # than silent. Computed directly off the corpus (independent of the agent's own digest build).
119	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_digest import _build_alias_map  # noqa: PLC0415
120	    _guard_stats: dict[str, int] = {}
121	    _build_alias_map(swg, evidence, stats=_guard_stats)
122	    doi_guard_hits = _guard_stats.get("doi_false_merge_guard_hits", 0)
123	    title_guard_hits = _guard_stats.get("title_false_merge_guard_hits", 0)
124	
125	    basket_ok = n_baskets >= min_baskets
126	    return {
127	        "corpus": corpus_path.name,
128	        "research_question": rq[:120],
129	        "baskets": n_baskets,
130	        "baskets_ok": basket_ok,
131	        "min_baskets": min_baskets,
132	        "cp4_used": cp4_used,
133	        "degrade_reason": degrade_reason[:200],
134	        "turns": oa.get("turns"),
135	        "ev_store_size": oa.get("ev_store_size"),
136	        "new_evidence": oa.get("new_evidence_count"),
137	        "quantified_models": len(qmodels),
138	        "calc_claim_sections": len(calc_claims),
139	        "moat_armed": moat_armed,
140	        "doi_false_merge_guard_hits": doi_guard_hits,
141	        "title_false_merge_guard_hits": title_guard_hits,
142	        "invariant_passed": passed and basket_ok,
143	        "verdict": verdict + ("" if basket_ok else f" | baskets {n_baskets} < min {min_baskets}"),
144	        "agent_model": outliner_agent_model(),
145	        "code_model": outliner_code_model(),
146	    }
147	
148	
149	def preflight_corpus(corpus_path: Path, min_baskets: int) -> tuple[bool, str]:
150	    """OFFLINE (no LLM) loadability check for one persisted corpus.
151	
152	    Exercises the exact offline-checkable portion of ``_run_one``: parse JSON, wrap clusters as the
153	    attribute-accessed objects ``build_outline_digest`` expects, count TOTAL baskets, and assert
154	    every ``member_indices``/``representative_index`` resolves into the attached evidence pool. This
155	    is what proves the corpus is key-drop-ready before credentials land. Returns (ok, message).
156	    """
157	    from types import SimpleNamespace  # noqa: PLC0415
158	    try:
159	        corpus = json.loads(corpus_path.read_text())
160	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
161	        return False, f"unreadable JSON: {exc}"
162	    for key in ("research_question", "evidence", "finding_clusters"):
163	        if key not in corpus:
164	            return False, f"missing required key {key!r}"
165	    evidence = corpus["evidence"]
166	    n_ev = len(evidence)
167	    raw_clusters = corpus.get("finding_clusters") or []
168	    clusters = [SimpleNamespace(**c) if isinstance(c, dict) else c for c in raw_clusters]
169	    n_baskets = _count_baskets(clusters)
170	    # every index a downstream consumer will dereference must be in-range
171	    bad = 0
172	    for c in clusters:
173	        idxs = list(getattr(c, "member_indices", []) or [])
174	        rep = getattr(c, "representative_index", None)
175	        if rep is not None:
176	            idxs.append(rep)
177	        for i in idxs:
178	            if not isinstance(i, int) or i < 0 or i >= n_ev:
179	                bad += 1
180	    if bad:
181	        return False, f"{bad} member/representative indices out of range for a {n_ev}-row pool"
182	    if n_baskets < min_baskets:
183	        return False, f"baskets {n_baskets} < min {min_baskets}"
184	    return True, (f"loadable: {n_baskets} baskets (>= {min_baskets}), {n_ev}-row pool, all indices "
185	                  "resolve")
186	
187	
188	def _dry_run_selfcheck(corpus_paths: list[Path] | None = None, min_baskets: int = 1) -> int:
189	    """Exercise the invariant machinery OFFLINE (no LLM, no keys) so we know the assertions are
190	    sound before credentials land. Four cases cover the whole decision surface. When ``corpus_paths``
191	    are given, ALSO preflight each corpus for loadability (parse, cluster-wrap, basket count, index
192	    resolution) so the mission's key-drop-ready proof runs offline against the real corpus."""
193	    cases = [
194	        ("agentic", "", True, "clean agentic path passes"),
195	        ("agentic-degraded-seed",
196	         "ReasoningFirstTruncationError: reasoning prelude exceeded completion budget",
197	         True, "sanctioned GLM-5.2 truncation degrade passes"),
198	        ("agentic-degraded-seed", "TimeoutError: agent wall exceeded", False,
199	         "non-truncation degrade FAILS"),
200	        ("plain", "", False, "fallback-plain FAILS"),
201	        ("MISSING", "", False, "missing cp4 block FAILS"),
202	    ]
203	    ok = True
204	    print("DRY-RUN self-check of the cp4_used invariant (_classify):")
205	    for cp4, reason, expect_pass, label in cases:
206	        passed, verdict = _classify(cp4, reason)
207	        mark = "OK " if passed == expect_pass else "BAD"
208	        if passed != expect_pass:
209	            ok = False
210	        print(f"  [{mark}] cp4_used={cp4!r:26} expect_pass={expect_pass!s:5} -> {passed!s:5} | {label}")
211	        print(f"        verdict: {verdict}")
212	    # basket counter check — TOTAL baskets = every cluster (singletons included)
213	    fake_clusters = [{"member_indices": [1, 2]}, {"member_indices": [3]}, {"member_indices": [4, 5, 6]}]
214	    nb = _count_baskets(fake_clusters)
215	    mark = "OK " if nb == 3 else "BAD"
216	    if nb != 3:
217	        ok = False
218	    print(f"  [{mark}] _count_baskets -> {nb} (expected 3: TOTAL clusters, singletons included)")
219	
220	    for cp in corpus_paths or []:
221	        if not cp.exists():
222	            print(f"  [BAD] corpus preflight: {cp} does not exist")
223	            ok = False
224	            continue
225	        cok, msg = preflight_corpus(cp, min_baskets)
226	        print(f"  [{'OK ' if cok else 'BAD'}] corpus preflight {cp.name}: {msg}")
227	        if not cok:
228	            ok = False
229	
230	    print("\nDRY-RUN", "PASSED — assertion machinery sound." if ok else "FAILED.")
231	    return 0 if ok else 1
232	
233	
234	def main() -> int:
235	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
236	    ap.add_argument("--corpus", type=str, help="a single corpus JSON")
237	    ap.add_argument("--corpus-dir", type=str, help="a dir of corpus JSONs (one run each)")
238	    ap.add_argument("--min-baskets", type=int, default=1,
239	                    help="assert each corpus has >= this many TOTAL baskets (default 1; the s3gear "
240	                         "full corpus is 329 baskets — assert it with --min-baskets 328)")
241	    ap.add_argument("--out", type=str, default="outputs/agentic_sweep/summary.json")
242	    ap.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
243	                    help="offline self-check of the assertion machinery (no LLM, no keys)")
244	    args = ap.parse_args()
245	
246	    if args.dry_run:
247	        _dry_paths: list[Path] = []
248	        if args.corpus:
249	            _dry_paths.append(Path(args.corpus))
250	        if args.corpus_dir:
251	            _dry_paths.extend(sorted(Path(args.corpus_dir).glob("*.json")))
252	        return _dry_run_selfcheck(_dry_paths, args.min_baskets)
253	
254	    # ── LAW VI: pin the seat + model lock for the sweep (env override still wins if pre-set) ──
255	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT", "1")
256	
257	    # ── HONEST BLOCKER: never fake a pass when the LLM cannot be called ──
258	    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
259	        print("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY is not set — the agentic outliner cannot call GLM-5.2.")
260	        print("  Present LLM-relevant env:",
261	              [k for k in os.environ if "API" in k or "KEY" in k or "TOKEN" in k] or "(none)")
262	        print("  This is the sole remaining gate for metric (a)-full-corpus. Run this exact command")
263	        print("  the instant credentials land; it will produce the cp4_used=agentic verdict per corpus.")
264	        print("  (Meanwhile: `--dry-run` proves the assertion machinery is sound offline.)")
265	        return 2
266	
267	    if os.environ.get("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT") not in ("1", "true", "True", "on"):
268	        print("BLOCKED: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT is disabled — refusing to run a non-agentic 'agentic' sweep.")
269	        return 2
270	
271	    corpora: list[Path] = []
272	    if args.corpus:
273	        corpora.append(Path(args.corpus))
274	    if args.corpus_dir:
275	        corpora.extend(sorted(Path(args.corpus_dir).glob("*.json")))
276	    corpora = [p for p in corpora if p.exists()]
277	    if not corpora:
278	        print("BLOCKED: no corpus given/found. Pass --corpus <file> or --corpus-dir <dir>.")
279	        print("  Build the full corpus first: python scripts/cp3_to_cp4_corpus.py")
280	        print("  then: --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --min-baskets 328 (329-basket corpus).")
281	        return 2
282	
283	    results = [asyncio.run(_run_one(p, args.min_baskets)) for p in corpora]
284	
285	    out_path = ROOT / args.out
286	    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
287	    n_pass = sum(1 for r in results if r["invariant_passed"])
288	    n_moat = sum(1 for r in results if r["moat_armed"])
289	    summary = {
290	        "corpora": len(results), "passed": n_pass, "failed": len(results) - n_pass,
291	        "moat_armed": n_moat, "results": results,
292	    }
293	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
294	
295	    print(f"\nAGENTIC SWEEP: {len(results)} corpus/corpora, {n_pass} PASSED the cp4_used=agentic invariant, "
296	          f"{n_moat} moat-armed")
297	    for r in results:
298	        mark = "PASS" if r["invariant_passed"] else "FAIL"
299	        print(f"  [{mark}] {r['corpus']}: cp4_used={r['cp4_used']} baskets={r['baskets']} "
300	              f"qmodels={r['quantified_models']} calc_sections={r['calc_claim_sections']} "
301	              f"moat_armed={r['moat_armed']}")
302	        # Observability: DOI/title false-merge REFUSAL count. Expected 0 on the real corpus; a
303	        # non-zero here means two multi-member cp3 works shared a DOI/title and the fold declined
304	        # to merge them — the zero-incidence assumption broke and warrants a key-level remap look.
305	        _dg = r.get("doi_false_merge_guard_hits", 0)
306	        _tg = r.get("title_false_merge_guard_hits", 0)
307	        _flag = "  <-- NON-ZERO: audit union-find assumption" if (_dg or _tg) else ""
308	        print(f"         false_merge_guard_hits: doi={_dg} title={_tg}{_flag}")
309	        print(f"         {r['verdict']}")
310	    print(f"\nsummary: {out_path}")
311	    return 0 if n_pass == len(results) else 1
312	
313	
314	if __name__ == "__main__":
315	    raise SystemExit(main())
316

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""The T2 edge-case battery runner (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md PART 3).
3	
4	Loads the battery cases (``tests/battery/cases/*.py``, each exporting ``BATTERY_CASES``), runs
5	them, ranks the failures by severity S0..S4, and writes the wheel's plain-text output:
6	  * outputs/battery/<run_id>/summary.json        — machine-readable per-case + per-severity totals
7	  * outputs/battery/<run_id>/ranked_failures.md  — S0-first human read (the wheel's routing signal)
8	  * outputs/battery/history.jsonl (append)       — one line per run: run_id, git sha, counts
9	
10	This first wave runs the DETERMINISTIC compute/faithfulness cases in-process, sequentially (they
11	are sub-second and share NO writable state; the one case that needs env seats sets+restores them
12	under a scoped context so the parent env is never left mutated). The ProcessPool + flock live-fetch
13	slots the design specifies are for the future AGENT-DRIVEN live cases (H01 targeted-fetch etc.),
14	which are not in this wave — that is called out honestly in the summary so the operator is not
15	misled about coverage.
16	
17	Usage:
18	  python scripts/outline_battery.py --all
19	  python scripts/outline_battery.py --only h01a_calc_render h01e_production_handoff
20	  python scripts/outline_battery.py --all --max-severity S1   # exit non-zero only on S0/S1
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import argparse
25	import asyncio
26	import importlib
27	import inspect
28	import json
29	import pkgutil
30	import subprocess
31	import sys
32	import time
33	from datetime import datetime, timezone
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
37	if str(_REPO) not in sys.path:
38	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO))
39	
40	from tests.battery.harness import (  # noqa: E402
41	    Assertion, BatteryCase, CaseResult, SEVERITIES, severity_rank,
42	)
43	
44	_OUT_ROOT = _REPO / "outputs" / "battery"
45	_CASES_PKG = "tests.battery.cases"
46	
47	
48	def _git_sha() -> str:
49	    try:
50	        return subprocess.check_output(
51	            ["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"], cwd=_REPO, text=True,
52	            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
53	        ).strip()
54	    except Exception:
55	        return "unknown"
56	
57	
58	def load_cases(only: list[str] | None) -> list[BatteryCase]:
59	    """Discover every ``BATTERY_CASES`` list under tests/battery/cases/."""
60	    import tests.battery.cases as cases_pkg  # noqa: PLC0415
61	
62	    cases: list[BatteryCase] = []
63	    for mod_info in pkgutil.iter_modules(cases_pkg.__path__):
64	        if mod_info.name.startswith("_"):
65	            continue
66	        mod = importlib.import_module(f"{_CASES_PKG}.{mod_info.name}")
67	        for c in getattr(mod, "BATTERY_CASES", []):
68	            cases.append(c)
69	    if only:
70	        want = set(only)
71	        cases = [c for c in cases if c.id in want]
72	    cases.sort(key=lambda c: c.id)
73	    return cases
74	
75	
76	async def _run_one(case: BatteryCase) -> CaseResult:
77	    t0 = time.monotonic()
78	    result = CaseResult(case_id=case.id, domain=case.domain,
79	                        capability=case.capability, xfail=case.xfail, note=case.note)
80	    try:
81	        out = case.run()
82	        if inspect.isawaitable(out):
83	            out = await out
84	        result.assertions = list(out or [])
85	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a probe crash is a case failure, never a wheel crash
86	        import traceback
87	        result.error = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}\n{traceback.format_exc()[-800:]}"
88	    result.wall_s = time.monotonic() - t0
89	    return result
90	
91	
92	async def _run_all(cases: list[BatteryCase]) -> list[CaseResult]:
93	    # Sequential: the deterministic compute cases are sub-second and one mutates process env under
94	    # a scoped restore, which is only safe without concurrent readers. Live agent cases (future
95	    # wave) get the ProcessPool.
96	    results: list[CaseResult] = []
97	    for c in cases:
98	        results.append(await _run_one(c))
99	    return results
100	
101	
102	def _rank_failures(results: list[CaseResult]) -> list[CaseResult]:
103	    """Failing (non-xfail) cases, S0 first; ties by case id."""
104	    fails = [r for r in results if r.outcome in ("fail", "error")]
105	    fails.sort(key=lambda r: (severity_rank(r.worst_severity or "S4"), r.case_id))
106	    return fails
107	
108	
109	def _severity_tally(results: list[CaseResult]) -> dict[str, int]:
110	    tally = {s: 0 for s in SEVERITIES}
111	    for r in results:
112	        if r.outcome in ("fail", "error"):
113	            tally[r.worst_severity or "S4"] += 1
114	    return tally
115	
116	
117	def write_outputs(results: list[CaseResult], run_id: str) -> tuple[Path, dict]:
118	    run_dir = _OUT_ROOT / run_id
119	    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
120	
121	    tally = _severity_tally(results)
122	    outcomes = {k: sum(1 for r in results if r.outcome == k)
123	                for k in ("pass", "fail", "xfail", "xpass", "error")}
124	    summary = {
125	        "run_id": run_id,
126	        "git_sha": _git_sha(),
127	        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
128	        "n_cases": len(results),
129	        "outcomes": outcomes,
130	        "severity_failures": tally,
131	        "note": (
132	            "Wave-1 deterministic compute/faithfulness cases (in-process). Agent-driven live-fetch "
133	            "cases (H01 targeted-fetch, H18 re-retrieval) are NOT yet in the battery — the "
134	            "ProcessPool/flock harness for them is future work."
135	        ),
136	        "cases": [r.as_dict() for r in results],
137	    }
138	    (run_dir / "summary.json").write_text(json.dumps(summary, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
139	
140	    ranked = _rank_failures(results)
141	    lines = [
142	        f"# Battery ranked failures — run {run_id} (git {summary['git_sha']})",
143	        "",
144	        f"cases={len(results)}  pass={outcomes['pass']}  fail={outcomes['fail']}  "
145	        f"xfail={outcomes['xfail']}  xpass={outcomes['xpass']}  error={outcomes['error']}",
146	        f"severity failures: " + "  ".join(f"{s}={tally[s]}" for s in SEVERITIES),
147	        "",
148	    ]
149	    if not ranked:
150	        lines.append("No S0/S1/S2/S3 failures. (xfail cases are known capability gaps, listed below.)")
151	    for r in ranked:
152	        lines.append(f"## [{r.worst_severity}] {r.case_id}  ({r.domain} / {r.capability})")
153	        if r.error:
154	            lines.append(f"- ERROR: {r.error.splitlines()[0]}")
155	        for a in r.failed:
156	            lines.append(
157	                f"- FAIL [{a.severity}] {a.name}: expected={a.expected!r} actual={a.actual!r}"
158	                + (f"  — {a.detail}" if a.detail else "")
159	            )
160	        lines.append("")
161	    xfails = [r for r in results if r.outcome == "xfail"]
162	    if xfails:
163	        lines.append("## Known capability gaps (xfail — not blockers)")
164	        for r in xfails:
165	            lines.append(f"- {r.case_id} ({r.capability}): {r.note or 'pending capability'}")
166	        lines.append("")
167	    xpasses = [r for r in results if r.outcome == "xpass"]
168	    if xpasses:
169	        lines.append("## XPASS — land these into the active set:")
170	        for r in xpasses:
171	            lines.append(f"- {r.case_id} ({r.capability})")
172	        lines.append("")
173	    (run_dir / "ranked_failures.md").write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
174	
175	    hist = {
176	        "run_id": run_id, "git_sha": summary["git_sha"],
177	        "at": summary["generated_at"], "outcomes": outcomes, "severity_failures": tally,
178	    }
179	    with (_OUT_ROOT / "history.jsonl").open("a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
180	        fh.write(json.dumps(hist) + "\n")
181	
182	    return run_dir, summary
183	
184	
185	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
186	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="T2 outline edge-case battery")
187	    ap.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="run every discovered case")
188	    ap.add_argument("--only", nargs="+", default=None, help="run only these case ids")
189	    ap.add_argument("--max-severity", default="S1",
190	                    help="exit non-zero if a failure at this severity or worse occurred (default S1)")
191	    ap.add_argument("--run-id", default=None)
192	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
193	
194	    if not args.all and not args.only:
195	        ap.error("pass --all or --only <case_id ...>")
196	
197	    cases = load_cases(args.only)
198	    if not cases:
199	        print("no cases matched", file=sys.stderr)
200	        return 2
201	
202	    run_id = args.run_id or datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
203	    results = asyncio.run(_run_all(cases))
204	    run_dir, summary = write_outputs(results, run_id)
205	
206	    tally = summary["severity_failures"]
207	    outcomes = summary["outcomes"]
208	    print(f"battery run {run_id} (git {summary['git_sha']}): {len(results)} cases")
209	    for r in sorted(results, key=lambda x: x.case_id):
210	        mark = {"pass": "PASS", "fail": "FAIL", "xfail": "xfail", "xpass": "XPASS",
211	                "error": "ERROR"}[r.outcome]
212	        sev = f" [{r.worst_severity}]" if r.worst_severity and r.outcome in ("fail", "error") else ""
213	        print(f"  {mark:5} {r.case_id}{sev}  ({r.wall_s:.2f}s)")
214	    print(f"outcomes: {outcomes}")
215	    print("severity failures: " + "  ".join(f"{s}={tally[s]}" for s in SEVERITIES))
216	    print(f"output: {run_dir}/ranked_failures.md")
217	
218	    cutoff = severity_rank(args.max_severity)
219	    blocking = sum(v for s, v in tally.items() if severity_rank(s) <= cutoff)
220	    if blocking:
221	        print(f"BLOCKING: {blocking} failure(s) at {args.max_severity} or worse", file=sys.stderr)
222	        return 1
223	    return 0
224	
225	
226	if __name__ == "__main__":
227	    raise SystemExit(main())
228

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""STEP 2 generality probe: call ONLY the outline SEED (_call_outline, one LLM call) on THREE
3	different-domain evidence sets and print the resulting section plan. Proves the structure fix is
4	TOPIC-DRIVEN and GENERAL (a topic-appropriate skeleton on AI/labor, medical, and finance) WITHOUT
5	running the full/expensive section-writing pipeline and WITHOUT tuning to any benchmark task.
6	
7	Run:
8	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
9	    PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1 \
10	        python scripts/outline_generality_probe.py
11	"""
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import asyncio
15	import json
16	import os
17	import sys
18	from pathlib import Path
19	
20	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
21	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
22	
23	
24	def _ev(eid, title, stmt, tier="T1"):
25	    return {"evidence_id": eid, "title": title, "statement": stmt, "tier": tier}
26	
27	
28	# --- Domain 2: MEDICAL (gut microbiota / intestinal function) — small synthetic pool ------------
29	MEDICAL = [
30	    _ev("gm_scfa_barrier", "Short-chain fatty acids and gut barrier integrity",
31	        "Butyrate produced by commensal bacteria strengthens the intestinal epithelial barrier and reduces permeability."),
32	    _ev("gm_dysbiosis_ibd", "Dysbiosis in inflammatory bowel disease",
33	        "Reduced microbial diversity and loss of Faecalibacterium prausnitzii are associated with Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis."),
34	    _ev("gm_fmt_cdi", "Fecal microbiota transplantation for C. difficile",
35	        "Fecal microbiota transplantation restores microbial diversity and resolves recurrent Clostridioides difficile infection in most patients."),
36	    _ev("gm_gutbrain_vagus", "Gut-brain axis signaling via the vagus nerve",
37	        "Commensal microbes modulate host behavior and mood through vagal afferent signaling and microbial metabolites."),
38	    _ev("gm_probiotic_rct", "Probiotic supplementation randomized trial",
39	        "A randomized controlled trial reported modest improvement in gastrointestinal symptoms with multi-strain probiotic supplementation.", "T1"),
40	    _ev("gm_diet_fiber", "Dietary fiber shapes microbial composition",
41	        "High-fiber diets enrich butyrate-producing taxa whereas Western diets deplete them.", "T2"),
42	    _ev("gm_immune_treg", "Microbiota and regulatory T-cell development",
43	        "Colonization by specific Clostridia species promotes regulatory T-cell differentiation and mucosal immune tolerance."),
44	    _ev("gm_metaanalysis", "Systematic review of microbiome interventions",
45	        "A systematic review found heterogeneous outcomes across microbiome-targeted interventions, limiting firm clinical recommendations.", "T2"),
46	    _ev("gm_antibiotic", "Antibiotic-driven dysbiosis and recovery",
47	        "Broad-spectrum antibiotics cause durable reductions in microbial diversity that recover incompletely over months.", "T1"),
48	    _ev("gm_gaps", "Open questions in microbiome causality",
49	        "Whether microbial changes are cause or consequence of disease remains unresolved in most human studies.", "T4"),
50	]
51	
52	# --- Domain 3: FINANCE (global insurers comparison) — small synthetic pool ----------------------
53	FINANCE = [
54	    _ev("fin_solvency_ratios", "Solvency II capital ratios of top insurers",
55	        "Leading European insurers reported Solvency II coverage ratios well above regulatory minimums, signaling strong capital buffers.", "T3"),
56	    _ev("fin_growth_5yr", "Five-year premium growth across major insurers",
57	        "Gross written premiums grew at differing rates across the ten largest global insurers over the past five years.", "T2"),
58	    _ev("fin_dividend", "Dividend payout and shareholder returns",
59	        "Several large insurers maintained stable dividend payout ratios and executed share-buyback programs.", "T2"),
60	    _ev("fin_china_potential", "Foreign insurer expansion in China",
61	        "Regulatory liberalization has expanded foreign insurers' access to the Chinese market, raising their growth potential.", "T3"),
62	    _ev("fin_credit_rating", "Credit ratings and financial strength",
63	        "Major insurers hold AA-range financial-strength ratings from leading agencies, reflecting low default risk.", "T3"),
64	    _ev("fin_underwriting", "Underwriting profitability and combined ratios",
65	        "Combined ratios below 100 percent indicate underwriting profitability among the strongest property-casualty insurers.", "T2"),
66	    _ev("fin_investment_income", "Investment portfolio and interest-rate sensitivity",
67	        "Insurers' investment income rose with higher interest rates, though asset-liability mismatches remain a risk.", "T2"),
68	    _ev("fin_esg", "ESG and long-term underwriting risk",
69	        "Climate and ESG exposures are reshaping catastrophe underwriting and long-term liability assumptions.", "T4"),
70	    _ev("fin_market_share", "Market share and geographic diversification",
71	        "Geographic diversification across Asia, Europe, and the Americas buffers insurers against regional shocks.", "T2"),
72	    _ev("fin_gaps", "Data limitations in cross-insurer comparison",
73	        "Differences in accounting standards complicate direct cross-border comparison of insurer financials.", "T4"),
74	]
75	
76	MEDICAL_RQ = ("Research the significance of the gut microbiota in maintaining normal intestinal "
77	              "function, its role in disease, and therapeutic interventions that target it.")
78	FINANCE_RQ = ("Collect and compare the world's top ten insurance companies across financing, "
79	              "creditworthiness, five-year growth, dividends, and future potential in China, and "
80	              "assess which are most likely to rank highest by assets in the future.")
81	
82	
83	async def run_one(name, rq, evidence, domain, model):
84	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _call_outline
85	    pr, retry, itok, otok = await _call_outline(
86	        rq, evidence, model, 0.2, 2500, domain=domain,
87	    )
88	    print(f"\n===== {name}  (domain={domain!r}, {len(evidence)} ev, retry={retry}) =====")
89	    print(f"ok={pr.ok}  n_sections={len(pr.plans)}  reason_codes={pr.reason_codes[:4]}")
90	    for i, p in enumerate(pr.plans):
91	        print(f"  {i+1}. {p.title}   [{len(p.ev_ids)} ev]")
92	        print(f"       focus: {str(p.focus)[:110]}")
93	    return [p.title for p in pr.plans]
94	
95	
96	async def main():
97	    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
98	        print("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
99	        return 2
100	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import outliner_code_model
101	    model = outliner_code_model()
102	    print(f"facet={os.getenv('PG_FACET_OUTLINE')}  skeleton={os.getenv('PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON')}  model={model}")
103	
104	    # AI/labor: sample from the real corrected corpus (keep it small for a cheap probe).
105	    corpus = json.loads((ROOT / "data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json").read_text())
106	    ai_ev = corpus["evidence"][:70]
107	    ai_rq = corpus["research_question"]
108	    ai_domain = corpus.get("domain", "workforce")
109	
110	    results = {}
111	    results["ai_labor"] = await run_one("AI / LABOR", ai_rq, ai_ev, ai_domain, model)
112	    results["medical"] = await run_one("MEDICAL", MEDICAL_RQ, MEDICAL, "medical", model)
113	    results["finance"] = await run_one("FINANCE", FINANCE_RQ, FINANCE, "finance", model)
114	
115	    (ROOT / "outputs" / "outline_generality_probe.json").write_text(
116	        json.dumps(results, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
117	    print("\nWROTE outputs/outline_generality_probe.json")
118	    return 0
119	
120	
121	if __name__ == "__main__":
122	    raise SystemExit(asyncio.run(main()))
123

1	"""OVH GPU topology + capacity probe (I-cd-008, GH#640).
2	
3	Read-only verification of OVH catalog + project-region capacity for the
4	locked Carney topology: 8xH200 (h200-1920) generator box + 4xH100
5	(h100-1520) evaluator box, in a non-US region (Canada BHS or France
6	GRA/SBG/RBX).
7	
8	Per operator memory `feedback_verify_primary_sources_before_relying_2026_05_15`:
9	- Cross-check via 4+ independent signals (catalog @ CA + catalog @ FR +
10	  project flavor list + per-region quota state).
11	- Per-region availability, not billing-currency. Catalog presence at a
12	  subsidiary is NOT the same as project-region availability or quota.
13	
14	Per Codex iter-2 brief P2:
15	- Use unfiltered `GET /cloud/project/{serviceName}/flavor` + client-side
16	  filter (region-filtered probes 404 on aggregate codes).
17	- Quota endpoint: `GET /cloud/project/{serviceName}/region/{regionName}/quota/allowed`
18	  (note the `/region/{regionName}/` prefix; NOT a project-wide path).
19	- `GET /cloud/project/{serviceName}/region` to derive real region names
20	  before per-region calls.
21	- `/cloud/order/rule/availability` as an additional catalog/order-rule
22	  signal (not cart/expressOrder dry-runs).
23	
24	Reads `OVH_*` from .env. NEVER ECHOES SECRET VALUES. Writes a JSON
25	audit artifact to `outputs/audits/I-cd-008/probe_result.json`.
26	"""
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import json
30	import sys
31	from datetime import datetime, timezone
32	from pathlib import Path
33	
34	import ovh
35	
36	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
37	ENV_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".env"
38	OUT_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "audits" / "I-cd-008" / "probe_result.json"
39	
40	# Locked Carney topology — operator-locked, do not negotiate.
41	TARGET_SKUS = ("h200-1920", "h100-1520")
42	GPU_SUBSTRINGS = ("h100", "h200", "l40", "l4-", "a100", "a10-", "gpu")
43	NON_US_REGION_PREFIXES = ("BHS", "GRA", "SBG", "RBX", "WAW", "ERI", "UK", "DE")
44	
45	
46	def _read_env(path: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
47	    env: dict[str, str] = {}
48	    if not path.exists():
49	        sys.exit(f"FATAL: .env not found at {path}")
50	    for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines():
51	        s = line.strip()
52	        if not s or s.startswith("#") or "=" not in s:
53	            continue
54	        k, v = s.split("=", 1)
55	        env[k.strip()] = v.strip()
56	    for k in ("OVH_ENDPOINT", "OVH_APPLICATION_KEY", "OVH_APPLICATION_SECRET", "OVH_CONSUMER_KEY"):
57	        if k not in env:
58	            sys.exit(f"FATAL: {k} missing from .env")
59	    return env
60	
61	
62	def _client(env: dict[str, str]) -> ovh.Client:
63	    return ovh.Client(
64	        endpoint=env["OVH_ENDPOINT"],
65	        application_key=env["OVH_APPLICATION_KEY"],
66	        application_secret=env["OVH_APPLICATION_SECRET"],
67	        consumer_key=env["OVH_CONSUMER_KEY"],
68	    )
69	
70	
71	def _catalog_skus(client: ovh.Client, subsidiary: str) -> list[dict] | dict:
72	    """Catalog presence at a billing subsidiary. Returns list of GPU plan rows,
73	    or a dict with `error` key if the endpoint rejects this subsidiary
74	    (e.g., querying `ovhSubsidiary=FR` against the `ovh-ca` endpoint)."""
75	    try:
76	        cat = client.get("/order/catalog/public/cloud", ovhSubsidiary=subsidiary)
77	    except ovh.exceptions.APIError as exc:
78	        return {"error": f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", "subsidiary": subsidiary}
79	    rows: list[dict] = []
80	    for addon in cat.get("addons", []):
81	        pc = (addon.get("planCode") or "").lower()
82	        if not any(k in pc for k in GPU_SUBSTRINGS):
83	            continue
84	        rows.append(
85	            {
86	                "planCode": addon.get("planCode"),
87	                "invoiceName": addon.get("invoiceName"),
88	                "blobs_tags": (addon.get("blobs") or {}).get("tags", []),
89	                "pricings_count": len(addon.get("pricings") or []),
90	            }
91	        )
92	    return rows
93	
94	
95	def _project_regions(client: ovh.Client, project_id: str) -> list[str]:
96	    """Real region codes enabled for the project (e.g., BHS5, GRA9, GRA11)."""
97	    return client.get(f"/cloud/project/{project_id}/region")
98	
99	
100	def _project_flavors(client: ovh.Client, project_id: str) -> list[dict]:
101	    """UNFILTERED project-wide flavor list (per Codex iter-2 P2)."""
102	    return client.get(f"/cloud/project/{project_id}/flavor")
103	
104	
105	def _region_quota(client: ovh.Client, project_id: str, region: str) -> dict:
106	    """Per-region quota state."""
107	    return client.get(f"/cloud/project/{project_id}/region/{region}/quota")
108	
109	
110	def _region_quota_allowed(client: ovh.Client, project_id: str, region: str) -> dict:
111	    """Per-region allowed-quota (what increases the project can request)."""
112	    return client.get(f"/cloud/project/{project_id}/region/{region}/quota/allowed")
113	
114	
115	def _order_rule_availability(client: ovh.Client, sku: str) -> dict | None:
116	    """Catalog/order-rule availability signal (per Codex iter-2 P2)."""
117	    try:
118	        return client.get("/cloud/order/rule/availability", planCode=sku)
119	    except ovh.exceptions.APIError:
120	        return None
121	
122	
123	def probe(env: dict[str, str], project_id: str) -> dict:
124	    """Run the full 4+ signal probe; return structured result."""
125	    client = _client(env)
126	    result: dict = {
127	        "probe_utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
128	        "project_id": project_id,
129	        "endpoint": env["OVH_ENDPOINT"],
130	        "target_skus": list(TARGET_SKUS),
131	    }
132	
133	    # Signal 1+2: catalog at CA and FR subsidiaries.
134	    result["signal_1_catalog_CA"] = _catalog_skus(client, "CA")
135	    result["signal_2_catalog_FR"] = _catalog_skus(client, "FR")
136	
137	    # Signal 3: project flavors (unfiltered, then client-side filter).
138	    # Both wrapped in try/except so a single failure doesn't abort the whole
139	    # probe (per Codex diff iter-1 P2).
140	    try:
141	        regions = _project_regions(client, project_id)
142	        result["project_regions"] = regions
143	    except ovh.exceptions.APIError as exc:
144	        result["project_regions"] = []
145	        result["project_regions_error"] = str(exc)
146	        regions = []
147	    try:
148	        all_flavors = _project_flavors(client, project_id)
149	    except ovh.exceptions.APIError as exc:
150	        all_flavors = []
151	        result["project_flavors_error"] = str(exc)
152	    target_rows = [
153	        {
154	            "name": f.get("name"),
155	            "region": f.get("region"),
156	            "available": f.get("available"),
157	            "quota": f.get("quota"),
158	            "ram": f.get("ram"),
159	            "vcpus": f.get("vcpus"),
160	        }
161	        for f in all_flavors
162	        if (f.get("name") in TARGET_SKUS)
163	    ]
164	    result["signal_3_project_flavors_target"] = target_rows
165	    result["project_flavor_total_rows"] = len(all_flavors)
166	
167	    # Signal 4: per-region quota + allowed-quota for candidate non-US regions.
168	    candidate_regions = [r for r in regions if any(r.startswith(p) for p in NON_US_REGION_PREFIXES)]
169	    result["candidate_non_us_regions"] = candidate_regions
170	    quota_state: dict = {}
171	    for region in candidate_regions:
172	        entry: dict = {}
173	        try:
174	            entry["quota"] = _region_quota(client, project_id, region)
175	        except ovh.exceptions.APIError as exc:
176	            entry["quota_error"] = str(exc)
177	        try:
178	            entry["allowed"] = _region_quota_allowed(client, project_id, region)
179	        except ovh.exceptions.APIError as exc:
180	            entry["allowed_error"] = str(exc)
181	        quota_state[region] = entry
182	    result["signal_4_region_quota"] = quota_state
183	
184	    # Extra signal: order-rule availability per SKU.
185	    result["signal_5_order_rule_availability"] = {
186	        sku: _order_rule_availability(client, sku) for sku in TARGET_SKUS
187	    }
188	
189	    # Topology verdict — derive from the actual target-SKU state, not hardcoded.
190	    # BOTH target SKUs must have at least one project-flavor row with
191	    # available=True AND quota>=1 for the topology to be obtainable.
192	    has_h200 = any(
193	        r.get("name") == "h200-1920" and r.get("available") and (r.get("quota") or 0) >= 1
194	        for r in target_rows
195	    )
196	    has_h100 = any(
197	        r.get("name") == "h100-1520" and r.get("available") and (r.get("quota") or 0) >= 1
198	        for r in target_rows
199	    )
200	    verdict: dict = {
201	        "target_skus_obtainable_now": bool(has_h200 and has_h100),
202	        "h200_1920_obtainable": has_h200,
203	        "h100_1520_obtainable": has_h100,
204	        "blockers": [],
205	    }
206	    if not has_h200:
207	        if not any(f.get("name") == "h200-1920" for f in all_flavors):
208	            verdict["blockers"].append(
209	                "h200-1920 absent from project flavor list entirely — operator must request "
210	                "OVH support add this SKU to the project allowlist for a non-US region "
211	                "(preferred: GRA9 or GRA11)."
212	            )
213	        else:
214	            verdict["blockers"].append(
215	                "h200-1920 present in project flavor list but no row has available=True AND "
216	                "quota>=1 — operator must request an OVH quota increase."
217	            )
218	    if not has_h100:
219	        if not any(f.get("name") == "h100-1520" for f in all_flavors):
220	            verdict["blockers"].append(
221	                "h100-1520 absent from project flavor list entirely — operator must request "
222	                "OVH support add this SKU to the project allowlist."
223	            )
224	        else:
225	            verdict["blockers"].append(
226	                "h100-1520 present in project flavor list but no row has available=True AND "
227	                "quota>=1 in any project region — operator must request an OVH quota "
228	                "increase (GRA9/GRA11 candidates)."
229	            )
230	    result["verdict"] = verdict
231	    return result
232	
233	
234	def main() -> int:
235	    env = _read_env(ENV_PATH)
236	    project_id = "446fccde73604cfbb0758c6012dad6d1"  # state/ovh_infra.md
237	    result = probe(env, project_id)
238	    OUT_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
239	    OUT_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str), encoding="utf-8")
240	    summary_lines = [
241	        f"probe_utc: {result['probe_utc']}",
242	        f"endpoint: {result['endpoint']}",
243	        f"project_regions: {', '.join(result['project_regions'])}",
244	        f"candidate_non_us_regions: {', '.join(result['candidate_non_us_regions'])}",
245	        f"signal_1 catalog@CA: {len(result['signal_1_catalog_CA']) if isinstance(result['signal_1_catalog_CA'], list) else result['signal_1_catalog_CA']}",
246	        f"signal_2 catalog@FR: {len(result['signal_2_catalog_FR']) if isinstance(result['signal_2_catalog_FR'], list) else result['signal_2_catalog_FR']}",
247	        f"signal_3 project flavors hitting target SKUs:",
248	    ]
249	    for row in result["signal_3_project_flavors_target"]:
250	        summary_lines.append(
251	            f"   {row['name']:>16} @ {row['region']:>8} available={row['available']} quota={row['quota']}"
252	        )
253	    summary_lines.append(f"verdict.target_skus_obtainable_now = {result['verdict']['target_skus_obtainable_now']}")
254	    for b in result["verdict"]["blockers"]:
255	        summary_lines.append(f"BLOCKER: {b}")
256	    print("\n".join(summary_lines))
257	    print(f"\nfull JSON: {OUT_PATH}")
258	    return 0
259	
260	
261	if __name__ == "__main__":
262	    sys.exit(main())
263

content.text: "Locked Carney topology" — "Carney" appears to be a proper noun (codename) for the topology configuration. Not flaggable per se as unprofessional, but let me note it as borderline. It's a project codename, acceptable. Continuing.

1	"""
2	Component-level verification for POLARIS graph SOTA features.
3	
4	Tests each feature with REAL calls, REAL data, and measures latency.
5	Must pass before committing to a full pipeline run.
6	
7	Usage:
8	    python -u scripts/pg_component_verify.py
9	"""
10	
11	import asyncio
12	import os
13	import sys
14	import time
15	
16	# Ensure project root on path
17	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
18	
19	# Load .env before any imports that read env vars
20	from dotenv import load_dotenv
21	load_dotenv(override=True)
22	
23	results = []
24	timings = {}
25	
26	
27	def report(name: str, passed: bool, detail: str, elapsed: float):
28	    """Record and print a test result."""
29	    status = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
30	    results.append((name, passed, detail))
31	    timings[name] = elapsed
32	    print(f"  [{status}] {name}: {detail} ({elapsed:.1f}s)")
33	
34	
35	# ============================================================================
36	# 1. PageRank API — real HTTP call
37	# ============================================================================
38	async def test_pagerank_api():
39	    print("\n=== 1. PageRank API (real HTTP call) ===")
40	    t0 = time.time()
41	
42	    api_key = os.getenv("OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY", "")
43	    if not api_key:
44	        report("PageRank API", False, "OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY not set in .env", 0)
45	        return
46	
47	    try:
48	        import aiohttp
49	        url = "https://openpagerank.com/api/v1.0/getPageRank"
50	        headers = {"API-OPR": api_key}
51	        params = [("domains[]", "google.com"), ("domains[]", "epa.gov"), ("domains[]", "example.com")]
52	
53	        timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=20)
54	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(timeout=timeout) as session:
55	            async with session.get(url, headers=headers, params=params) as response:
56	                elapsed = time.time() - t0
57	                if response.status != 200:
58	                    body = await response.text()
59	                    report("PageRank API", False, f"HTTP {response.status}: {body[:200]}", elapsed)
60	                    return
61	
62	                data = await response.json()
63	                items = data.get("response", [])
64	
65	                # Validate we got scores
66	                scores = {}
67	                for item in items:
68	                    domain = item.get("domain", "")
69	                    score = item.get("page_rank_decimal", 0)
70	                    scores[domain] = score
71	
72	                google_score = scores.get("google.com", 0)
73	                epa_score = scores.get("epa.gov", 0)
74	
75	                if google_score > 0 and epa_score > 0:
76	                    report("PageRank API", True,
77	                           f"google.com={google_score}, epa.gov={epa_score}, "
78	                           f"{len(items)} domains scored", elapsed)
79	                else:
80	                    report("PageRank API", False,
81	                           f"Scores look wrong: {scores}", elapsed)
82	
83	    except Exception as e:
84	        elapsed = time.time() - t0
85	        report("PageRank API", False, f"Exception: {e}", elapsed)
86	
87	
88	# ============================================================================
89	# 2. LettuceDetect GPU inference — load model + run prediction
90	# ============================================================================
91	async def test_lettuce_detect():
92	    print("\n=== 2. LettuceDetect GPU inference (real model load + predict) ===")
93	    t0 = time.time()
94	
95	    if os.getenv("PG_HALLUCINATION_DETECT_ENABLED", "0") != "1":
96	        report("LettuceDetect", False, "PG_HALLUCINATION_DETECT_ENABLED=0 in .env", 0)
97	        return
98	
99	    try:
100	        # This triggers model download + load onto GPU
101	        from src.polaris_graph.agents.hallucination_detector import _get_detector
102	
103	        t_load_start = time.time()
104	        detector = _get_detector()
105	        load_time = time.time() - t_load_start
106	
107	        if detector is None:
108	            report("LettuceDetect", False,
109	                   "Model failed to load (check GPU/CUDA availability)", time.time() - t0)
110	            return
111	
112	        # Run actual inference on a known hallucination
113	        context = [
114	            "Water filtration using activated carbon removes chlorine and VOCs. "
115	            "Reverse osmosis removes up to 99% of dissolved salts and contaminants. "
116	            "PFAS contamination affects over 2,800 communities in the United States."
117	        ]
118	        question = "What are effective water filtration methods?"
119	        # This answer contains a hallucination: "quantum filtration" is made up
120	        answer = (
121	            "Activated carbon removes chlorine effectively. "
122	            "Reverse osmosis removes 99% of contaminants. "
123	            "Quantum filtration using nanotube resonance chambers eliminates all PFAS instantly."
124	        )
125	
126	        t_infer_start = time.time()
127	        predictions = detector.predict(
128	            context=context,
129	            question=question,
130	            answer=answer,
131	            output_format="spans",
132	        )
133	        infer_time = time.time() - t_infer_start
134	        elapsed = time.time() - t0
135	
136	        if predictions and len(predictions) > 0:
137	            # Check if the hallucinated span was detected
138	            flagged_text = " ".join(p.get("text", "") for p in predictions)
139	            has_quantum = "quantum" in flagged_text.lower() or "nanotube" in flagged_text.lower()
140	            report("LettuceDetect", True,
141	                   f"Model loaded ({load_time:.1f}s), inference ({infer_time:.1f}s), "
142	                   f"{len(predictions)} spans detected, "
143	                   f"caught fabrication={'YES' if has_quantum else 'partial'}",
144	                   elapsed)
145	        else:
146	            report("LettuceDetect", False,
147	                   f"Model loaded ({load_time:.1f}s) but detected 0 hallucination spans "
148	                   f"on known-bad input (inference {infer_time:.1f}s)",
149	                   elapsed)
150	
151	    except Exception as e:
152	        elapsed = time.time() - t0
153	        report("LettuceDetect", False, f"Exception: {e}", elapsed)
154	
155	
156	# ============================================================================
157	# 3. Crawl4AI — real page fetch
158	# ============================================================================
159	async def test_crawl4ai():
160	    print("\n=== 3. Crawl4AI (real page fetch) ===")
161	    t0 = time.time()
162	
163	    if os.getenv("PG_CRAWL4AI_ENABLED", "1") != "1":
164	        report("Crawl4AI", False, "PG_CRAWL4AI_ENABLED=0", 0)
165	        return
166	
167	    try:
168	        from src.tools.access_bypass import AccessBypass
169	        bypass = AccessBypass()
170	
171	        # Use a stable, Unicode-heavy page to test both fetch AND Unicode fix
172	        test_url = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PFAS"
173	        result = await bypass._try_crawl4ai(test_url)
174	        elapsed = time.time() - t0
175	
176	        if result and result.success and len(result.content) > 500:
177	            report("Crawl4AI", True,
178	                   f"{len(result.content)} chars from Wikipedia PFAS "
179	                   f"(no Unicode crash on Windows)", elapsed)
180	        elif result and not result.success:
181	            error = result.metadata.get("error", "unknown")
182	            report("Crawl4AI", False,
183	                   f"Fetch failed: {str(error)[:200]}", elapsed)
184	        else:
185	            content_len = len(result.content) if result else 0
186	            report("Crawl4AI", False,
187	                   f"Insufficient content: {content_len} chars", elapsed)
188
